park_name: BIG4 Park Lane Noosa North Shore

reviews.review_text: The staff were so helpful, nothing was a problem. Coffee and the food were delicious . Our cabin was clean and very comfortable. We couldn’t fault a thing.
It was great

Stayed for a weekend break, with my daughter, son in law and two grandchildren. We had a fantastic time, there is plenty to keep the children ( and the grown ups) happy. Mini golf, bouncy pillow, small amusement arcade, swimming pools, climbing walls, cycling with a great track. Plenty of bbq areas as well. Great location, some lovely walks, to the lakes and a short drive 5 mins to the beach. We had a great time - thanks

We stayed in a glamping tent. It was beautiful. We had a lovely outlook of the bush, the 'room' was clean and beds were comfy. Didee at reception and the other staff went out of their way to make it a really nice family holiday for us. The park has lots of activities for kids but it still felt like we got a nature experience - we appreciated that there wasn't too much concrete and we got to see a few roos and goannas.

Another great stay, staff very friendly and very welcoming from the start to the end. Facilities are very clean and tidy and the whole atmosphere is Family friendly. Thank you to the team at Noosa North Shore for another great stay.

Nice holiday park.
Loved the glamping tents and the sound of the ocean.

The activities area needs attention and Renovations. Putt putt course not good.
We always enjoy staying at your camp site. Staff is always friendly and very helpfull.
We had an amazing trip with lots to do, can’t wait to come back again!
See you again next year!
Really one of the best camping sites, we have been. Very family friendly with so many activities. It’s important to plan and book in advance for the paid activities

Thanks Noosa Northshore. Our group had a great time at your came grounds. Pity the weather made it very wet for the weekend but would highly recommend and sure to be back at some stage.
Regards Brian

Family-friendly should not mean unfriendly to non-families, but this is sadly what we experienced here.

My partner and I regularly visit this area and usually camp in the national park. Last week after a few nights camping in Teewah, we wanted a little 'glamping' experience on our last night with a pub meal and a hot shower. We booked at Big4/Park Lane and never received a confirmation email. When we pulled into our site, we were shocked at the chaos around the campground. Understandably busy during school holidays, but the scene we were greeted with was unnecessarily loud, with out of control children everywhere, parents nowhere to be seen.

We went to dinner and were thoroughly unimpressed with our meals. My partner's chicken schnitzel had a chicken-nuggety processed texture. We reserved a table in the bar area, assuming it'd be quieter than the bistro area where we expected families to be sitting (near the big screen playing Moana and the colouring-in table), but for some reason the people seated next to us seemed to find their small children playing around/under our table a more suitable option.

When we returned from dinner, it was past 8pm, and large groups were still struggling to set up their tents in the darkness, shining their flashlights into our site and all around. There were children running through our site, tripping over our stuff. Nearly every campsite had some sort of large TV or projector setup, with loud speakers blaring shows, movies, and video games.

There is limited phone reception in the campground (to be expected) so we would have had to hike back to reception/bistro area—which we just came from—to make an after-hours complaint regarding this chaotic scene. Instead, promptly left to camp elsewhere and did not end up staying in our site.

This seems like a lovely holiday park for parents who want to drink at their campsites and go off-duty while they let their kids run amok.

Great place to camp for a few days or more.
We had a powered site, they are a bit close together is my only criticism
Very clean facilities and friendly staff
Great location if you wanted to head off up the beach for the day.

We recently stayed at Noosa North Shore for the Easter weekend. When we booked for Easter we were staying there in December and asked for the same site we were currently in. They said that would be no problem. We checked prior to our stay that the booking was still good and that we were located close to our friends but they informed us we had been move as a larger group wanted to be together. This was really disappointing to hear that we didn’t have our site but also that we weren’t close to our friends any longer. We asked them to fix this which they kindly did and relocated us both to be closer but not side by side. When we arrived we found our site was the smallest site and we couldn’t even open our awning without hitting the caravan beside us. So again we spoke to reception and they luckily had a cancellation so moved us to a bigger site. The only issue now was the new site was completely inundated with ground water and our whole site was muddy and underwater for days. Water would literally come through our mat and into our shoes when we walked through our awning area. This was a nightmare to manage with kids and trying to keep the van clean inside. What was worse there is a water catchment behind this site where the vans above sent their grey water. So it makes sense that is what we were trudging through for days. So gross! We spoke to reception again and they said they’d had a lot of rain lately and that’s normal but no other areas were soggy like this. There is clearly a known drainage issue in those sites and those sites shouldn’t be hired out until it’s fixed in my opinion. Finally after Easter Monday the park cleared out and we were able to move again  to our original site that we booked in the first place. It took us most of the day packing up, moving and then setting up again. So losing a day for that was disappointing and we had the pleasure of paying $180 for the privilege. We had a much better experience from then on. The most disappointing thing was there was no offer of compensation whatsoever. We felt like we were “those people” always complaining. We had the worst experience for the first half of our holiday and we didn’t feel heard or respected by the park. The staff are polite and friendly in general but really don’t seem to have any awareness of how problems like this impact families.

We celebrated a joint 50th at the resort with about 45 people.  It was the perfect base for our celebrations. Special thanks go to Tracey and Jenni for all their help organising.  Such a great place for a big group of kids and adults and everyone had an amazing time.
We just visited Parklane over the Easter weekend! Shout out to Oli on reception who went above and beyond making us feel very welcome. 5 stars!
It was an amazing camp site and great for families and kids with lots of activities.

Stayed for Easter weekend
For a big4 this place was very tired
Road unkept and very rough with a lack of lighting needed a touch every time.
The part we where in had one block of toilets but no showers you had to walk to the other side of camp to use the showers which where old with paint peeling off walls and worn through on floors.
15% surcharge on all four days
2 cappuccinos over $13
2 bowls of chips $27
For what we paid being a long weekend we should have gone elsewhere.
Great stay! Clean facilities, perfect location, and a really relaxing "vibe". Looking forward to coming back.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ (4/5) – Beautiful stay with a few “high-maintenance glamper” upgrades suggestions.

We recently stayed in the glamping tents and overall had such a amazing experience — this is definitely somewhere we’ll be coming back to every year!

The setup itself is stunning. The outdoor eating and sitting areas were perfect for relaxing, especially in the mornings and evenings. The whole campground is really well maintained, and having your own allocated car space right outside the tent is super convenient. and **DOG FRIENDLY**, which was a huge win for us. Our German Shepherd absolutely loved it 🐾

The ferry access (as of March 2026) is easy enough, just something to factor into your trips back and forth.

“High-Maintenance Glamper” suggestions

For $200 a night, I do think there are a few small improvements that would elevate the experience — but the good news is, you can easily work around them:

Lighting
The main tent has gorgeous LED strip lighting, but the bathroom only has one dim light. Extending the LED lighting into the bathroom would make a huge difference.

Bunk Bed Area
* The table in the corner doesn’t really serve a purpose → swap this for a small camping wardrobe/closet
* The chairs are bulky and just get in the way → we ended up moving them

Kitchen Setup
The fridge is only a bar fridge → very small
* Don’t buy ice and stick to 1L milk cartons (door space is narrow)
* Bringing a water filter (GAME CHANGER) saved fridge space
* A portable gas stove is 100% worth it
A simple **$2 dish rack** makes washing up so much easier

The Weber outside works, but for proper cooking (like pasta), this is way better - Bring a pot and a pan they don't have any from memory. Also helps avoid mozzies at night

Layout Tweaks
We moved the big couch closer to the bed with the table in front — worked way better for the space.

Sand Situation
No matter how much you try… sand gets everywhere.
→ Could they put  a rug under the bed that would look amazin
or bath mats for beside the bed = lifesaver

We added candles along the wooden ledge above the bed — super cute and cozy at night

Storage
There’s not much storage, so bringing or the site making a small closet setup makes a big difference for longer stays.

A full-length mirror would be a bonus - GRWM out fit check !

Connectivity
Phone reception is patchy
WiFi is available but not secure
No clear emergency contact access while on-site (that we could find), which could be improved

Ratings Breakdown
Room: 4/5
Beautiful, but lacks storage and a few practical touches for longer stays

Service: 5/5
The staff were absolutely amazing — so kind and helpful. Only downside is finding a contact number while on-site

Dining/Cafe
A bit overpriced and closes around 7pm
Food at their reception - general supplies are double nearly triple store prices so make sure you stock up BEFORE crossing the ferry.

Location: 4/5
We went back to Noosaville 2–3 times during the week (ferry costs add up)
Not a huge amount to do on Noosa North Shore for adults
Very family/kid-friendly
We mostly enjoyed walks and beach time

Final Thoughts
If you’re staying for a weekend — you’ll have no issues at all.
If you’re staying longer (like we did for a week), just come prepared with a few extras and you’ll have the best time.

Despite my “high maintenance” adjustments 😅 — we absolutely loved it and will be back every year.

Hope you liked this review - hope it was helpful
enjoy your stay it really is a stunning camp site !
Un camping très agréable en pleine nature. Il est grand et on se trouve assez éloigné de la réception. Mais la nature est magnifique 🤩
Great weekend away with the the family
Loved every second of it. Great staff, great area, great rooms! Couldn’t fault! Will definitely be coming back again

We have been coming to Noosa north shore since my early 20s, Large camp sites with clean facilities.
The pool as always needs attention perhaps even a brand new one.
It's a great place to break away from the noise and relax. Our kids loved the small pump track, jumping pillows and mini golf.

The main campground is beautiful and the bathrooms well equipped. The glamping is well designed and we managed to see the inside as friends of our were staying in them. They are quite pricey. We stayed in the lakeside cabins and the experience was disappointing. The units were clean but very outdated. The bathrooms are gross. The charges for these are high too. There was something wrong with our hot water cylinder and we had to have cold showers. I am afraid we wont be back.
Great camping had a beaut time and dog friendly ! Awesome👍👍👍👍👍👍😃

Currently staying in a glamping tent which is fine but my low star review is because none of the advertised activities are available to us! It should be better communicated that the park's activities such as mini golf, climbing wall etc are not available for paying guests when there are school camps on site. So definitely not hours of fun for everyone!! Had we known this, we likely would have booked elsewhere. The jumping pillow is also deflated and unusable and we were kicked off the obstacle course near the games room! Time to upgrade your website because it's currently highly misleading.
had a great stay, staff were really helpful :)

Great experience! Gentleman at reception on my check in was really helpful and lovely! We had dinner and cocktails at the pub and it was awesome too, service was fast and food was good, again the gentleman was really kind too all the kids we had, they had a ball in the arcade. Thank you!

We booked over the phone as we are travelling and we were unaware of a $32 each way fare on top of an $89 site fee it was disgusting when we have been stay free in some places with better services. And then there is a lack of showers (5) and toilets that you have to walk in mush to get to. Most disappointing stay and will not return or recommend to anyone.

We booked for 7 nights in the van park but left early, we were on site 28.
The powered sites are all over the place with not enough water and power for the amount of sites. You need  double hose and double power lead to plug in. The sites run parallel to the road. Unless you are on the end sites good luck getting in and out. The amenities we cleaned once in the 6 days we were there. The park is very dated. The pool needs repairs. It has bore water only. We had dark brown water go through our van which needed to be flushed out as they had issues with the water. For $87 a night on Friday and Saturday $70+ Sunday and $60+ through the week it is expensive. For a Big4 park I  expected better.
Ensuite campsite was in great clean condition. Camp-site was clean and tidy and all staff were amazing

We stayed in glamping tent 142. It was in a  perfect location, and very relaxing. Would love to have had a security screen door that could be locked, only the glass doors were lockable. Shame that behind us there was a large group of people camping who were extra loud til 1am on the first night we stayed.

The lawns and grounds were well maintained and lovely to see all the bird life, kangaroos, possums and goanna!

We’d def love to come back and stay once again!

The standard room for 7 is very very average nothing like the pictures. Reception were amazing but the whole experience so far…….. haven’t even spent one night………. is disappointing. There is no phone service and only access to wifi at the main reception area. We had no gas working and no remote for the tv and for all these issues had to walk to reception to complain. We were offered an upgrade however this was a family trip for our parents 50th Wedding Anniversary so we wanted to stay close to them. Their room on the other hand was lovely so we will be spending most of our time with them. For reference they booked the family cabin!

Reservamos a precio de 90$ un sitio pero no sabiamos que habia ferry y no llegabamos al ultimo. Llamamos para cancelarla y nos dijeron que si que nos devolverían todo el dinero, pero mas tarde nos dijeron que se quedaban 50$ por la cara. Muy mal servicio y mal gestionado por su parte ya que no tienen en cuenta el ferry, solo piensan en el dinero. Sin ni poder ir nos cobraron 50$.

We had a lovely stay at Park Lane Big4 Noosa North Shore.
After a terrible situation of another Holiday House Big4 Staff helped us out with our last minute Accommodation without any hesitation.

The Big4 is well organised, family-friendly, & very welcoming. The Staff provide clear information for everyone to enjoy their stay, & the facilities are very clean & well maintained.

The Park feels very safe, with regular patrols & very helpful after-hours support if needed.

It’s fantastic that the Place is Dog-friendly,. Parking is easy, & the layout of the whole place made it simple to get around.

A relaxing, well-run place with a great atmosphere — perfect for families, couples, & anyone looking for a comfortable & enjoyable stay. We would definitely happily return here.

All in all a great stay for a large group over the long Australia Day weekend.

Can Paige’s are nice and spread out and plenty of good amenities and things to do with a great location close to beach driving at Double Island.

Only disappointment was the power going out a number of times throughout the weekend across the whole site. However, I guess this was because of people powering up AC’s due to it being very warm and power cuts didn’t last too long

Overall great stay at Park Lane Noosa North Shore.  Great activities for kids, amenities were ample and reasonably clean. Restaurant is great and they had a fair bit of stuff in the general store.

Down sides: we had a powered site but there's only 1 electrical tower shared with a lot of sites and it was very far from our site.  I had a 10m lead but that wasn't enough, I had to daisy chain off the neighbours which was probably not super safe to have power boards chained off power boards.

Being a public holiday over school hols it was obviously busy, so much so the power system couldn't keep up and we had a couple of blackouts.  Thankfully I had a backup battery otherwise we might have had to throw out some perishables in the fridge.  They did offer to store perishables at reception but that's a major pain.

The markings of where each site starts and ends were pretty unclear so we just had to kind of work it out.  I can see it being a source of arguments if people are more headstrong/less considerate.

Overall would definitely come back.  I hear new management is likely to be improving things even further.

As a first timer visiting a Big 4 site, I was super impressed by the openness and space on the land. However my check in experience was basic, a map and a key. Wasn't told about any extra information, activities for children, putt putt, bike riding, restaurant hours or bookings etc. And I did mention on check in that it was first time at a Big 4.

I would recommend bringing lots of food over to the site as the shop there has little fruit, veggies or healthy snacks.

Overall a beautiful space out in nature.

The room had a light fixture hanging from the ceiling when it shouldn't have been , so they promptly had an electrician check and fix it . Within the first hour of stay . The plumbing in the 2nd bathroom thumped on the wall and the toilet gargled so we didn't use it .
I felt let down by staff other than a few that were just nice and did the  job well with a smile.  As service management seemed annoyed when I had asked to honour the tag price of a holiday gift , it scanned higher and I was told the price tag is not correct as they  are still working on it .
The amount of time I saw guests step out of the staff way or the amount of staff flock to the bar ahead of guests was a surprise. Staff socialise in shift time and guests wait.

Kids holiday program was not mentioned  at all until I spotted a sign , but was promptly told all the reasons why  they don't go ahead . The menu was very creative and the children 's menu was overpriced and embarrassing.
We used the pool a lot which was clean and maintained.

Great place — our family had a wonderful time there.
It’s a very good spot for kids.

El lugar es perfecto. La ubicación es un poco alejada del centro y toca pasar en ferry. Pero es súper práctico pasar y el servicio al parecer está diaponible todo el día. Cuesta 16 aud cada tramo. Pero el lugar está increíble. Vimos canguros 🫶. Las instalaciones súper limpia. Más que recomendable. Perfecto 👌. Solo podemos decir que le faltaba horno a la cocina.

We enjoyed our stay at Big4 Noosa North Shore. The Deluxe Powered Site was very roomy however lacked shade. The park itself has lots of amenities and things to do for the kids and it was a big pro that we could bring our dog. The restaurant/pub was great and the cafe had a great selection of food/drinks. Would recommend.

Bathroom door not on runner.  Half shower screen missing.  Whole area poorly maintained.  Received email that the cabin would be serviced and was not.  Asked the next day for fresh towels which weren’t delivered until we called again.  Roads and pathways on property poorly maintained.  All 4 of us are not keen to revisit.
Absolutely beautiful definitely will be back with family.

Not much parking when arriving, place seems a bit run down but could be because of the rain. Not sure I would be back here. Kitchen is pretty far from where we were. There needs to be more bins around as it's a bit of a walk to find a bin. Laundry machines take forever to accept card not sure if that has anything to do with no internet but took me a good 8 times untill my card was accepted. No light around at night. Entry could be maintained a little better. The place needs a good clean and some TLC and I'm sure it will get there as I have read that the place has recently been bought by other company.

This park is a fantastic Bjg 4 park for families. We stayed in the 4 bedroom cottage which was situated in a great spot close to the Tavern for early morning coffee! The outdoor patio area was so good and having 3 x pools in the whole complex saved overcrowding. The adults & kids loved the Laser Tag and kids rock climbng & bike tracks too. Not to mention the beautiful beaches only 1.5kms down the road!

We stayed here last year, not much has changed visually in a year to be honest.
I did camp this time so cannot confirm if the cottages have been updated.
Bathrooms in the camping and restaurant area require more frequent cleaning please. I was rather shocked a few occasions.
Menu can be changed, options are ok, meals are hit and miss, drinks like cocktails are luck of the draw in terms of taste for the price.
We had sorted our sites but had campers turn up to our spot, the new staff on the reception desk didn’t know anything about it so communication is poor.
Great location. Issues with power which impacted on showers/hot water.
Other than the power issues our stay was  great 👍

Camp ground was clean and tidy, amenities were clean.
Not convenient if not camping close to bathrooms. I found that the amenities were far away. The camp kitchen did not have hot water.
Due to the amount of people on the first night we had no power for several hours.
Staff were friendly. Everything activity costs money, not explained very well on the website.

We had 2 nights in a glamping tent and thoroughly enjoyed it.
Thanks Noosa North Shore

We booked our stay on the day we arrived and were charged $250 per night for a povered site. I understand January is busy, but the site we received was muddy, uncomfortable and not worth the price. Amenities were dirty and the staff both on the phone and at reception were unfriendly and unhelpful.
We asked for a refund because we literally had to stay in the mud, but were told it wasn’t possible. Very disappointing experience.
Was disappointed with stay...left a day early than booked for....

The room was a little shabby. The walls were cracked, the water was yellow and the kitchen was tiny. It had 7 beds which was great but too small for cooking a Christmas dinner. The place is nice but a little run down.
We came late. No lights on the site. Coudnt find No 39 site. There was No power anywhere on this site. The pool was under maintenence. So No - this Place is not to be prefered again
Our second time visiting and we never want to leave, the park is beautifully maintained facilities are great, staff are so wonderful. We will be back again soon. Thank you!

This park is lovely if you love wild life .
And walking not good for small children or the elderly!
The pools are very hard to enter and only small so very busy

We experienced the Glamping tents at Noosa North Shores and they were BEAUTIFUL! So clean and just lovely! We absolutely loved our walk home from the restaurant along the beautifully lit pathway back to the tent in the evenings. The wildlife didn't dissapoint and the beach at the end of the road was magic!
Great location, large park lots of space, short walk to cafe/pub. Toilets/ showers could have been cleaner. Make sure you have lots of mozzie spray, we will be back, with our van.

The location is great. The environment is really nice and the staff very helpful.
But .... our cabin was very dusty when we entered. We had to broom the floor and there were a lot of dirt sitting on the floor. TA couple of towels had stain marks. This was disappointing. I would rate 3.5 stars, but the smile of the staff led us to the rating.
Very nice place to camp

Yes not happy about the ferry not mentioned $64 for return joirney very expensive .We left two days early as storm hit no showers  no toilet flushing  no laundry  or power on powered site intersting that I  fouid one tap labled hot that was working some what in the landry. NO BACKUP GENERATOR FOR EMERGENCY .Nothing offerted no refund just oh well i live on site and i havent had a showet either "comment from staff . All in all as no  backup generator or disclosure of ferry prices per vehicle on booking woulld not reccommed this park .

We highly recommend the BIG 4, Noosa North Shore. We recently had an exceptional experience there, and everything about our stay was tremendous.

The amenities are excellent and cater to children of all ages, including pools, a bouldering wall, arcade area, and paddle boards. The rooms were modern and clean, the staff were incredibly helpful, and the location offered true tranquillity.

I highly recommend Noosa North Shore to anyone seeking a relaxing, fun-filled, and enjoyable stay for their family.

The Tent site was large and  flat, with power close by and water available in the nearby communal BBQ area which has 3 large BBQs. There was also a convenient sink there. The showers were excellent and soap and toilet paper etc was all available. We had a fire (need a raised firepit). It was great for our family with kids. It was only a couple minute drive to the ocean and a beautiful beach. It was a great weekend.

We had a lovely weekend at Noosa North Shore Big4. We met the owner and received wonderful service on all fronts. The owners have only recently taken over and have great plans to freshen the place up which will make the accomodation even better. The location is so beautiful and gardens/grounds are kept really nice. We had a wonderful time - the kids particularly loved the bouldering wall and pool.

The weather was amazing with thunder storms and spectacular lightning but we were safe and dry in our beautiful, clean cabin that had everything we needed. The next light the light drizzle did not stop us having a fun drive on the beach and a swim. Would love to visit again

BIG4 Noosa was just what we needed, some time away to relax in a beautiful, peaceful and scenic wonderland.  We were so thrilled to be able to enjoy firsthand the birds and wildlife.  Would and have already recommended to friends.
I sent an email as per the advice that ‘if you not happy with stay’ email us but hadn’t received a response so thought I’d then post on here.

We stayed on a powered site. The shower and toilet facilities were filthy. There was cotton buds all over the women's showers at 4pm, they were still there at 6am! You had to queue to have a shower even at 6am!

The men's facilities were just as bad,  extremely muddy, and it hasn't rained.

The site is huge and caters for large school groups,  which is the reason the facilities were dirty.

It is near a beautiful beach and kangaroos visit the site at dusk.

We have travelled from Cairns to Brisbane and this is our last favourite site.  Although if you have children there are lots of things to keep them entertained.
Found it Expensive to be able to go across the river on days we didn’t want to be on camp and explore.
Fantastic place, hidden Gem,  ill be back soon, staff are brilliant.
$70 for an unpowered spot in a crowded holiday park. Below average camp kitchen
Absolutely brilliant! Will definitely do it again. The sounds the wildlife.

Der Campingplatz ist sehr Gut 👍
Leider ist der Service zu weit weg Ca 1km
Auch der Beach ist Ca 1.5 km weit weg.
Gut ist das mann Kängurus und viele andere
Wild Tiere wie Kakadus zum greifen nahe sind.Auch die sanitären anlage sind sehr gut.

We camped here in early October and can’t rate it highly enough. Reception was excellent, amenities were clean, our site was private and spacious and there was a lot of birdlife. Taking a ferry to get there creates a great feeling of being away from it all although it’s still so close to Noosa. The park has a beautiful complex that contains reception, a restaurant and a shop.
Absolutely 💯 fantastic only down fall was the bed felt like it had Bars or ridges in them.
ROOMS AND SERVICE WERE GREAT. MEAL WAS GOOD.
I love camping here! Clean, well maintained.

Great caravan park! Close to all you need. We had an ensuite site and ensuite was clean with modern fixture.
The only thing that I wasn't happy with was the site. Too elevated for a 8.5m Caravan. Also I had to clean 2" of bark and leaf litter off the site before able to setup.
But once set up had a great stay with the grandies.

I have stayed here twice in last couple of months and the location, facilities and especially the smiley, happy staff make it a joy to visit. Would recommend to anyone looking for a relaxed, fun, comfortable, nature based getaway. Michael

⭐ Worst experience ever — do NOT recommend!
This was by far the worst experience I’ve ever had. The staff were extremely rude and unhelpful, and the customer service was terrible. I never received an email confirmation for my booking, and then I got a text at 8:36 pm saying my father hadn’t arrived — even though he’d already checked in at 1:30 pm! It caused unnecessary panic and stress. I kept receiving multiple text messages even after I confirmed that he was there. The prices were also completely overpriced for the poor service we received. Absolutely disappointing from start to finish.
Big 4, more like Big 4ish. Don't know how they got there banner. Very very run down.

Camping waar je alleen kunt komen met de Ferry. Goede camping waar je soms de kangeroes tegenkomt. Wel een eind lopen naar de receptie en het winkeltje. Toiletten en douches zijn prima. Weinig tot geen informatievoorziening over de regio bij receptie. Zoek het zelf maar uit.  Als              " foreigner" komt dit wel heel vreemd over en getuigt niet van service. Geen tot weinig WiFi op kampeerplek.

Very disappointing experience... will not be back. Actually left a day before we needed to. Reception staff need to work on their customer service skills... not welcoming at all. People who moved into the glamping tent next to us, were loud and obnoxious.... not considerate of others around them at all.

no wireless internet and difficult to get phone service unless you're at the one and only general store. You have to pay $30 each way to get across the ferry with a caravan. it was like being stranded on an island. Urinals out of order. no green grass. No peaceful sleep due to loud birds throughout every night. great secluded beach though!

We enjoyed our stay. Thankyou for your prompt service regarding our ensuite not being cleaner prior to our arrival. The issue was health with quickly.
We loved having the pool to use but would offer a suggestion that some rails be installed for better access to the pool. The slops into the pool are very steep and with some of us having slight disabilities (knee issues) it would have made it so much easier and safer.
Regards Toni

The family had a great time at the Big4 Noosa North Shore. We had a lakeview unit and whilst the room setup is basic we found it perfectly fine for two adults for four kids. All of the staff were great, very friendly and happy to answer any questions. Lots of activities for the kids and a great bar/resturanton on site. Only advice, bring bug spray for the evenings. Overall it's a great location, we'll be back.

Did not find the place clean and dogs not under control one had the run of the place , toilet and showers not clean it was peak time so extra cleaning should be done for the extra charges can't believe no dump site is provided giving the chance to use your own toilet also camp kitchen is very basics and out of action while private function was been held to me that's selling something twice not again for us get real

Very loud park people up all night yelling. Very uneven camp-site and the site we had was tiny we only had just enough room to put our ute on it to set up our rooftop tent with our 2 camp chairs right on top of our ute but if we were staying longer then 1night we would have wanted to set up our awning, table, cooking gear and single swag for our kid if she was to come with us which we would never have been able to due to our camp-site being way to small. We won't be back anytime soon. Also you barely get any phone service at your camp site so if you need to use your phone your best bet would be to do the hike to the reception area to use their wifi.

Stayed in an ensuite site in the campground for 3 nights. Ensuite was well appointed and super handy to have our own private bathroom. Campground is well maintained with large sites (ours was anyway). Great facilities with plenty for the kids to do. Friendly service, and the food at the smokehouse was decent. We will be back.

Was a great location but staff like eveyone else has written don’t really care, they had a special for adults only wings and wine at the restaurant but when I asked if the kids could have wings it was a huge deal and I overheard the staff say ti the chef “you know the person who has been asking a million questions about the wings” which I found rude. I then had a baby cino burn my daughter’s mouth where she was crying and she’s quite tough. For them to do it again the next day and make burning hot even though you told them all I got was a “sorry about that”. They couldn’t get the movies to work on the projector and it was undercover not an “outdoor movie” and staff asked if anyone could hotspot which was an bit unprofessional, very overpriced restaurant. Started cleaning the pool at 8am on the hottest day and made the kids get out.
Great place, great location, close to the beach, staff are friendly, facilities are clean and tidy. We have been a few times now and will be back soon
A great holiday location for large groups and families. This truly is a hidden gem, felt like stepping into another world. Close to nature with modern comforts- wonderful

Great Location for camping near amenities and kids were wrapped with the whole campground.
Staff fantastic.
Looking forward to coming again
Just a lovely place to get away for a few days.   Plenty of wildlife.  Good people and nice and quiet.
No dump point for rv or caravan users! Would not have booked if I had known. Have to travel down the road in vehicle to use public dump point.

None or very little WiFi or even TV which was frustrating and annoying. The beach, dump points and the entrance building where the park, bar, dining and sign in areas is, are all around 1k away in 2 different directions. a 700m walk from the park to the main entrance area is a stretch if you are older or incapacitated.
At $86 per night plus $64 for the ferry if you are towing makes it a very expensive exercise.. I would not go there again for free.
Great location!

The Staff are incredible at Noosa North Shore - The rooms are lovely and the food and beverage is incredible - Try the Steak its to die for..... Great Music in the Pub and the team make you smile all the time - well worth a visit.  Also it was lovely listening to the sound of the ocean roaring in the background

Caravan park sites need a bit of upkeep as does the campers kitchen. Nice spot, quite peaceful during the week but maybe overpriced for what you are offered - some slab sites very cracked and on arrival covered in mud and leaf litter and roads through the caravan park rough. Bathroom facilities good. Would be fine if the price reflected the state of the park.
Great location, beautiful setting and great staff

Its not very clear when booking online that there is a ferry needed to get here which will cost you $32 one way with a caravan so $64 for a round trip. Not the best location if you are wanting to explore noosa and surrounds. The caravan sites were really tiny, our caravan bumper was 1 foot off the drawbar of the neighboring caravan, how would they get out if they were leaving earlier than us? We had 3 nights booked but left after 1.There were also school camp groups here , when we were in the games rooms with our kids it was over run with school kids, the same with the pump track. The play ground, jumping cushion, mini golf, bouldering room etc were really far away from where the campsite. The amenities were nice and clean and grounds well maintained.

I think we found a new happy place!

The quality of amenities in the glamping setup was second to none. The serenity was delightful and the local area is just idyllic.

Already planning our next visit - couldn't have enjoyed our weekend escape any more.

Would be a nice place if you just want to relax and have all your necessities with you. We booked not knowing about the ferry till after the fact , which cost us more then our 3 nights accommodation. Should be made aware when booking. Booked. Premium slab site and it was all cracked and broken and the sites are in a weird angle and we had trouble getting our van out when someone camped next to us. Overall a bit disappointing we only stayed 2 of our 3 nights.

We camped with dogs on a slab site at the top. It was terribly laid out with people setting up smack bang on top of you so the at it made it extremely hard to hitch up and leave as we had trouble with the space between our hitch and the van set up in front of us. We couldn’t hook up to water as our hose didn’t reach where we needed to put it and we had a long hose. It was very noisy at the top with the road directly behind us and people coming in and out and driving to the toilet block.

We did love some of the grassed sites below and would happily stay there next time. The weather wasn’t great but it wasn’t too bad, we would come back now we know the layout.

IF YOU ARE GOING TO STAY HERE AND ON A BUDGET PLEASE FACTOR IN THE PRICE OF THE FERRY!

Me and my partner didn’t and ended up booking for 5 nights. If you want to do anything other than stay at the campsite all day, it’ll cost you $32 a day to return!

Also - with all that said we’ve stayed at previous Big4’s and loved them, but here, considering the price you have to pay it’s just not worth it at all!

Especially when the male amenities were being cleaned for 2 hours (6am-8am), probably the worst possible time as everyone wants to get up and shower for the day.

They opened a different block of amenities, but have the cheek to expect you to pay EVEN more money just to clean yourself🤣

It’s ridiculous and unfortunately REALLY made us consider staying at another BIG4 as we’re not quick to complain unless needed.

Writing this review as I hoped I had seen one earlier so hopefully it helps others :)

Great camping area and not very busy while we were there which we appreciated. Beautiful surroundings with plenty of birdlife and the kangaroos come out at night. Also close to beautiful beaches. Unfortunately the free WiFi was only available while near the laundry and not from our caravan which was a bit of a pain. Also, the renovated male showers have only one hook per cubicle for clothes and towel while the old one has three per cubicle. How much does a hook cost? And when other cubicles are in use the water pressure goes down dramatically and it’s get hard to adjust the temperature. Overall I still feel very positive about the place but there’s room for improvement….

It’s a nice campsite - good facilities and bbq area. We were a bit disappointed when we arrived at 4:30 that everything is totally closed at 4 - the shop and bar/restaurant. It seems odd that the bar and restaurant only open at weekends when it’s school holiday time. We rented a SUP (quite pricey at $50 for the day without option for half day) and realised the lake was around 700m away so we had to struggle there and back with the heavy things, I’d suggest a shuttle buggy for guests as it was really unmanageable and it wasn’t advised to us that it would be / that a truck would be useful to get there when we rented them. It’s a decent campsite but not standout.
Facilities clean and tidy, property beautiful! Staff were a little unhelpful. But overall a good experience!

Rooms: N/A- we had a site.

This wasn’t a great experience for us. When we booked, we were led to believe there was internet (which was importantly for us because work commitments). However, upon arrival, there was no internet on our site and only in reception, which was not specified on the website. We went to the restaurant and were met with an extremely rude woman who ruined the night for us, to top it off our food was appalling and terrible quality. We were then place on a wet and boggy site, which was so bad, we were forced to buy tarps to stop the mud oozing through our grass mats.. The location was fine, but there were no outstanding features so needed to leave for experiences so had the added expense  of the ferry every-time we wanted to leave the island, which wasn’t ideal. Aside from this, the bathrooms and showers were clean, other campers were lovely and everyone very respectful.

My family spent 8 nights (split between my husband and daughter then son, daughter-in-law and a 4 and 2 year old) in the wonderful expansive caravan park then 3 of us in a cabin for 4 nights. We loved the vast grassy areas of the park area, games room, great showers, the freedom of the park, and close proximity to the Retreat facilities and our cabin over the last 4 days.  Having great bike and walking tracks right beside the facilities was a major bonus. We found the staff to be extremely helpful, pleasant and the food at the pub great and convenient. Being an adventurous family, we found this to be a wonderful stay, driving on the beaches, surfing, and with positive whale viewing too, it was all brilliant. Thank you so much!
This is a great park for families who want to commune with nature and be off the grid.

Room was clean, pub had a lot of activity kid can do. But internet signals were really bad, where we stayed, the cottage had no internet, you had to walk out for some distance to call or to message someone. That was a big downside of our experience.
Wonderful location with Friendly staff and great food on offer, I highly recommend staying here
Fantastic holidays for families especially with kids and big groups..
Nice camping place, good showers, kids facilities, just outside town, close to the beach, very expensive compared to surroundings campings.

Truly an amazing location. A great group of people working throughout the place.  It’s a little dated, and as we stayed during school holidays, we’ve paid quite a premium for a powered site.  Wouldn’t recommend, if you are planning to use this as a base to see the sites of the Sunshine Coast as the ferry is now $38 return, but perfect if you want to spend a few days in nature
The only issue we had was a family set camp VERY close. They had a tent and a gazebo and we could reach both from our caravan windows.

We had a great Long weekend on a caravan site with a slab near the entrance.  Toilet facilities excellent and the bar at reception had a great selection of loc beers and the smoked bbq ribs were superb.

Nice location but for the price ($130/night for a grassed, powered site), if not roads paved with gold, I at least expected them paved with something other than gravel and potholes.

The new amenities block had nice tiles, and the water was hot (until someone flushed the loo), but a single hook to hang things on, nothing to rest anything like soap or shampoo on in the shower, and taps that fall off in your hands does not a great experience make.

We didn't use the camp kitchen, but compared to other parks we have stayed in, it was very basic.

Lack of dump point would be a nuisance if staying longer than a few nights. And if you usually go for a concrete pad site, don't bother (see pics).

We took the stroll (700 metres) from the park to the 'on-site' bar/bistro. The service was friendly, but the food was forgettable.

The outlook and trees were great, and seeing a few wallabies grazing was a nice touch.

Would have been a 3 star if the price was more in line with the standard provided.

Receiving 6 emails at the time of booking, another 9 a few days before arrival, 2 on the day before departure and 1 after departing was a bit over the top (yes, that's 18 emails in total). And getting two text messages telling me to complete the mandatory pre check in (with a link that took me to a completely different caravan park), make me think that the North Korean hackers had become involved.

A winter gateway with all the adventures. Allllllll 7 kids were entertained with the Giant jumping pillow, rock climbing and beach swims. Bbq breakfast, lunch and dinners were also a win. Our two bedroom with both rooms with ensuite plus public amenities was also a win. Pool side view, what more could a parent ask for? We will definitely be back.
Had the best time. Very peaceful.

Staff and facilities where fantastic.
Can't wait to take the grandkids

We loved our stay at Noosa North Shore. The meals at the pub were amazing and the staff are great and friendly. The only thing I think would need attention is that there are no bedside tables to put your things on and our door to the shared living space wouldn’t lock so if anyone else was in the adjoining room they would have been able to get into our room.

We had an average stay. The entire park had been booked by a school group so we were told we could not use any amenities in that section including the mini golf and bike track. We were not informed of this on booking. The camp area where we were staying also had tree lopping and mulching almost the entire time we were there which was incredibly loud.
Could have been a great stay but we had a bad experience.

The camping ground is awesome. Very nice views, quiet and peaceful.
The bathroom facility is new and very nice.
However the camping kitchen is terrible. A real disaster for a big4. Super disappointed about that. Very old and lack of equipment. No stove to cook...
On the day before we arrive I called to check the check in time and I was told 11am. However on the day we arrive we had to wait until 1pm. Very unpleasant surprise!
Some kids activities were out of order as well...
Overall it was good but would think twice before booking next time.

My family came and stayed for 2 nights last weekend in a 2bedroom cottage and we had an amazing time. The manager Tracy was very welcoming and made us feel right at home. We did have an issue with our TV but maintenance was onto it straight away and replaced with a brand new TV. The family activities around the park were amazing and the kids had such a great time. We played mini golf, rock climbing wall and laser tag and the kids couldn’t stop talking about it. The staff were wonderful when we came up for our morning coffees and drinks throughout the day. The kids loved spotting all the kangaroos and birds around the park. The cottage was very clean and tidy with more than enough towels and blankets for our family of 5. We will definitely be coming back often
Great spot, plenty of room for family wild, and cleanest camp amenities I ever did see 😜
Excellent experience! Would highly recommend! The cabin was awesome & lots to do, thank you 😌

We camped here as a part of a large group booking. We've camped at many places before but never have I come across such lovely staff at a campsite. ALL staff were very kind, relaxed and helpful. It was a fun and convenient place to camp. Super clean facilities, close to beach and lots of kangaroos and rainbow lorikeets that would land on our heads. Thanks for a great weekend

Excellent communication, great friendly service at reception and the Bar/Bistro. Bike track and laser tag were a hit!
Camp ground has excellent facilities.

The campground amenities were fantastic and very clean.
I wouldn’t recommend eating at the pub. The meals were over priced and underwhelming.
I ordered smoked salmon on Asian salad and got 50gms of shredded salmon on a supermarket salad with not a hint of the advertised coriander or Asian flavours. $30 value nah.

We came with around 20/30 school families for our yearly camp. It was our first time visiting this big 4. Kids and adults had all a great time. We enjoyed the outdoor by exploring the tracks, biking and walking. We went to double island with our cars driving on the beach and also surf at the pristine local beach. The sunset on the river was spectacular!
On site there were multiples choices of accomodations from cottages, glamping and camping. The pub was perfect to feed us and relax.
The kids had plenty things to do to keep entertained fron laser tag, climbing wall, mini golf, bike tracks, pools, jumping pillow… you can’t be bored there!
Everyone from their teams were welcoming and happy to help.
We are looking forward to our next visit.

Lovely stay with our family. A really family friendly family park, facilities were great, shop was well stocked, restaurant food was wonderful. Kids were well entertained even in the rain, climbing wall, laser tag, and jumping pillow did the job well. Cottage well located and grounds had plenty of nature to enjoy including families of kangaroos. Staff were well informed and very friendly, even the short ferry trip across the river to get there was fun.|Overall great weekend at reasonable price.
We stayed in a Glamping tent for 3 days. It was perfect 👍👍

Highly enjoyed our stay here. What a beautiful peaceful place. All staff are very friendly and helpful. Our glamping tent was amazing, spotless and couldn't fault a thing.
We will be back..

We have a great weekend with friends! The staff were so friendly, the facility were clean and well maintained. There was so much wildlife around!
The restaurant and kids jumping pillow were a hit by all! To top it off we were luckily enough to get a late check out allowing us to spend more time with friends and have some tasty smoked brisket burgers and pizzas! We will certainly be back… Soon 😀👌

We had the best stay in the Glamping tents for the weekend! They are beautifully set out and in such a peaceful spot. Highly recommend for a quiet weekend and quality time with loved ones. We also enjoyed the laser tag, so much fun! The ferry was super quick and easy and worth it to get to such a perfect location. Also enjoyed the most perfect beach without the need for a 4wd. We will be back!
We had our own caravan. The amenities, grounds and vicinity to the beach is great.
Good location would be good if path from camping ground to restaurant was walkable , water logged needs to be better set up so you can walk if wet

The room was old school . But had everything, we needed. There was a terrible smell in the lounge room,  ( smelt like cat piss) . There was old socks under the bed from previous tenants. And we had a major  problem with ants in the kitchen. After seeing the Glamping up the road, ( still under the same Big 4 ownership)That was much cleaner and well presented . I would definitely recommend that over the pool views cabins . Staff were awesome, the meals were ok.

Firstly we were shocked at the cost of the ferry to get here, so we were stuck on the island for 2 days because we couldn’t afford to get back over again and explore Noosa as we had planned, we thought we would go play some games with the kids (mini golf, laser, high rope, rock climbing) but it was way to expensive for 5 to do anything. So we had to sit in our van for 2 days! The sites are crammed in, you can’t fit a bigger van + your car.
Having to stay a minimum 2 nights was very expensive and a waste of time and money. We were expecting a nice stay here but unfortunately very dissatisfied & I hate leaving bad reviews.

Had a great weekend at one of the Ensuite sites. Customer service couldnt have been better.  Urgently needed a hose and the team went above and beyond to help me out.
Good shop onsite and bar/restaurant.  Has everything you need.
Thanks for a great weekend.
brilliant cabins spaced far enough apart kangaroo's an kookabaras 4x4 track on beach easy so perfect

Another great stay at  Noosa North Shore. Tried clamping for the first time and it was great especially with the rain we had.

We love staying here, NNSR and the staff never disappoint.

Tried the clamping tents this weekend and it was a fantastic experience. First time we have ever tried clamping and it was something we will do more often in the winter months. Our tent was very clean and spaces fitted for 4x adults and one child comfortably. As always the park and facilities were 5star. Thanks again NNSR for another great weekend.
Wir haben uns ein Glamping Zelt gemietet. Es war einfach nur wunderbar schön .Die Speisen im Restaurant sind optisch und geschmacklich super . Wir kommen gerne wieder !!!!

A beautiful bush camp. New amenities,  new flaming tents, staff are all happy , cheerful and a pleasure to deal with. The meals at the pub were yummy, coffee is great.
Loved all the kangaroos and goannas walking around.
Beach is great for driving on and fishing.
I highly recommend this park
Stayed in one of the Glamping tents. Absolutely beautiful and had everything we needed in there like cutlery and ketel etc. Just wish we had somewhere where we could hang up coats/jackets.
Stayed in the glamping tents 10/10! Loooovely bed

We had the most amazing stay at Noosa North Shore Retreat! We stayed in one of their glamping tents, which were incredibly spacious and had everything we needed for a luxury staycation. The whole experience felt like the perfect escape, plus it’s pet friendly so we could bring the fur babies with us. There are so many beautiful places to explore — we hired surfboards and caught some waves, drove along the 4WD beach, and ended our days watching the sunset over the lake.
Highly recommend this hidden gem!

This was our first time camping here and it was a pretty nice relaxing week.. was veryyyyy busy with Easter at the beginning of the week but thinned right out afterward..  we enjoyed the week alot until we copped the big storm Thursday night and ended up leaving a day early cause the ground was just soooo wet and sludgy, we couldn't even sit outside but that was out of everyone's control and we still enjoyed the week away..

Everything perfect!!!

Good spot, toilets cleaned, good location
Great cocktails

We have stayed at the retreat about 15/16 times over the last 5 years and Big 4 have made huge improvements to the park. The rain screwed us this time, but aside from that, a wonderful park. If you think its exy, Big 4 justified the price increases by plowing money into the infrastructure. Good work!
Beautiful quiet park. Lots of wildlife and lovely reception, bar and customer service

Our caravan site was great (site71) nice and level and a great out look over the park. Very clean facilities, friendly staff, close to the beach but also wind protacted for that great camp fire at night. Pub not too bad either, cold beers, footy and a feed if you like!

Camped on a non-powered site.  The camp grounds are big and beautiful with lots of trees, although the section in which we camped had no trees.  There's an ablution block with toilets only to service the bottom part of the camp, but it is quite a walk to the ablution block with showers.  Never had to wait for a shower and the water pressure is not too bad and I didn't once had to take a cold shower.  The beach is not too far, but you'll have to drive to get there.  There's a pool at the camp grounds though that's lovely.  The lake is walking distance, around 1.5km.
We really enjoyed our time there and I would recommend it to family and friends.
An Easter family get together at Noosa nth shore big 4 we all had a grand time and hope to back again soon to make more family memories

Great camping grounds. Clean shower and toilets., only wish the wi fi was working. Not good for keeping in contact when needed.
Hopefully that will be rectified. Staff so helpful.

After changing to big 4 this place has gone downhill we have been staying here for many of years over Easter and other times
Toilets where not kept clean multiple times there was toilets left with piss on the floor/seats in both male and female toilets
Showers where hot and cold at anytime of day/night
Expensive to stay here over 500$ for 4 nights and site are not level at all for delux or caravan sites
The pub was a complete disappointment
2x chicken parmigiana and 2x drinks over $90
And tiny serving and poor quality
I think I’ll try the water front camo next time

Stayed for Easter long weekend with extended family- Food at the pub was terrible and the steak was near inedible- tried to complain and they couldn’t have cared less and didn’t even apologise, the location is very mediocre with very limited things to do for young families and the facilities are very run down, the pools are outdated and absolutely freezing, wont be back ever again. I probably would have had a more enjoyable stay in social housing in Logan.
A great place for a family getaway together. A few bits and bobs around the property weren't working but it didn't hamper our enjoyment.

We were in a two bedroom cabin. The cabin was clean and pleasant with fresh linen and towels. Bed and pillows, more comfortable than many a well known hotel. The kitchen was basic but had all we needed. The plumbing was old and tired with new washers needed on the taps and bathroom renovation due.
The reception and bisto area was lovely and the staff all delightful, patient and well trained. Their experience with children was evident and the range of activities on offer during the Easter school holidays fantastic. Friendly kangaroos, impressive Lace Monitors (goanna) and lots of other bird life.
Wifi range needs to be extended.
We will be back.
Favourite Big 4 park so far! Activities are awesomen

We had a great stay, the bar and restaurant was great, the facilities were clean, the staff were friendly. Location was awesome and not too far from the beach. Only thing was the mozzies but that’s to be expected anyway. Will definitely be returning soon!

We thoroughly enjoyed our stay at Big4 Noosa North Shore. Such a beautiful location filled with greenery and wildlife.
The cottage we stayed in was adequate and clean.
The grounds and facilities were well kept. Staff kind and friendly.
Seeing the kangaroos morning and afternoon were our highlight.
We definitely will return.

Absolutely fantastic!!! We had such a great stay here - the service is phenomenal. We had an issue with our TV, which the management were quick to act on AND with a smile on their face. Nothing was ever too much trouble. Great rooms, heaps of activities for the kids! Will stay again!!

The powered site in which we stayed at had not been mowed and it was extremely muddy and disgusting. Ankle deep muddy water on thick grass. Horrible. Then they decided to mow the day before we were due to check out, before more arrived and expected people to set up on muddy sites. The games area where there is a shared kitchen, tables, giant chess and other things for kids to play was so outdated and the floors and everything were just grosse. Staff were friendly- no complaints there but the activities are so over priced. Other big4 parks don't charge extra, or at least definitely not that much like this place. Drainage is also terrible when it rains there. So many puddles,  the entrance towards the toilets near the bike track get full of muddy water, the entrance between the rooms outside the games room gets flooded that it almost reached the room entrances. It was not good. They need more concrete slabs and better maintenance of the grass and driveways. Also, the egg in the breakfast roll tasted like plastic. It was horrible.
Relaxed atmosphere, good food at the pub, plenty to do and unbelievably friendly and helpful staff

Another fun stay at Noosa North Shore, love all the activities on offer, the mountain bike track at the Caravan Park was a huge hit. Love the wildlife, the ducks in the dam, the kangaroos feeding at sunset, the cockatoos and the goannas! We love staying here - only a small negative: we were caught coming back from dinner at the restaurant in the dark, used to take our torches but because we saw the lighting had been put in along the track, we left them in the van, was a funny walk back in the dark dodging came toads!

Not great at all compared to other big 4 parks. Caravan/camping sites are a long way from the main area that has the jumping pillow etc and the roads in the park are all gravel/rock with plenty of washouts so not enjoyable or easy for the kids to ride bikes around. Nothing much around other than the beach and to go back to mainland to explore costs $30 return for the ferry. Minimal reception too. Definitely won’t be returning to this park.

We stayed here for a couple of nights.  While we were expecting the accommodation to be fairly basic - it is a campsite after all - it could really do with some money being spent - not just on the cabins, but across the whole place.  The crazy golf has seen better days and the pool was a bit grotty, no sail over the kid's pool and just a couple of loungers to sit on.  Some low level lighting to lead the way from the restaurant back to the cabins when it gets dark would be helpful.  Quiz night was good fun and the laser tag was a big hit.  The food was okay and drinks reasonably priced.

We stayed in the pool view rooms, we arrived at 14:00 and at approximately 14:30 the pool closed for maintenance which with 3 excited children was a major inconvenience. The pool did not open until the next morning. The pool fence needs maintenance for safety purposes mostly all of the fence is held up by star pickets. The rooms are extremely dated especially for the price. The bathroom sink did not drain it seemed clogged when filled with water.
On arrival the rooms were mostly clean except the floors had leaves and sand everywhere. Only a small face soap per person was supplied, there was zero body wash, shampoo or conditioner. The inside dining table is falling apart very unstable. 2 chairs for the outside table not 6 like there should have been, so not all of us could sit outside to enjoy a meal. For dinner we went to use the bbq but one of the BBQs isn’t working.
There is plenty for children to do but mostly everything comes at an extra cost.
There isn’t a daily fresh towel service.
We had lunch at the restaurant and the meals were average at best.
Overall our stay was a major disappointment and unfortunately definitely didn’t make us want to return and given it’s beautiful location That’s such a disappointment.
Thank you Danny, Jenny , Mara and all the wonderful staff for giving my son the most amazing birthday weekend!  🎉 Candice, Rees & family

Amazing place to stay for a camping trip.
We thoroughly enjoyed the activities available for the kids too. Also very close to Noosa Heads.
Staff we're very friendly and helpful, big shout out to them for their hospitality.
The glamping tents are a great option.
Would definitely consider going here again!

Beautiful location, so peaceful and away from the rat race, beautiful clean rooms, open spaces in the grounds, friendly staff and prompt personal service, great food, loved every aspect of this place, can't wait to come back 🥰

We had my 50th Birthday at Noosa North Shore retreat and the staff, managers and all involved made it the most amazing time we have ever had. They bent over backwards to help accomodate every need. The food was amazing, the set up was beautiful and the rooms were fresh and clean with enough space for all of us to stay comfortably.
I cannot thank you all enough.
Highly recommend Noosa North Shore Retreat to everyone. Massive 5 stars 🙏

Rooms have improved but the bathrooms require a complete overhaul. Kitchen spacious but also requires an upgrade. Plenty of space and good for families with good BBQ area. Restaurant food still poor which is a shame however the coffee is good.
Location was sensational amenities were in good clean condition diction all team memebers were helpful and friendly a nice park

Glamping tents…. AMAZING! I will be coming back. Noosa North Shore provided us with a fantastic comfortable setup for a family of four. From comfortable beds to our own ensuite. The addition of real plants made for a real homely experience. Just remember to bring your own little bbq to cook on your private balcony and you’re all set.

Wir waren mit einem Wohnmobil unterwegs und hatten eine Powered Site für mehrere Übernachtungen gebucht. Leider wurden wir sehr enttäuscht ! Der Platz ist schön, aber voller Mängel. Für so einen großen Platz gibt es viel zu wenig Toiletten für Männer. Noch dazu war im Sanitärbereich immer etwa ein Viertel defekt. Die Camperküche ist sehr mangelhaft eingerichtet. Es fehlten ein Warmwasserkocher, ein Toaster und andere Einrichtungsgegenstände über die eigentlich jeder Campingplatz in Australien verfügt. Es gibt kein warmes Wasser zum Spülen und die Grills sind in einem schlechten Zustand. Das Dach ist zu knapp bemessen und bei Regen wird die Hälfte der Sitzgelegenheiten nass. Eine eigene Dumpstation ist ebenfalls nicht vorhanden ! Am Pool bröckelt der Zement . . .
Als man uns dann eines Morgens ohne Vorwarnung Wasser und Strom abgestellt hatte, haben wir uns beschwert. Die Reaktion des Managements empfanden wir als arrogant und überheblich. Offenbar ist man dort nicht sehr interessiert, seine Gäste zufriedenzustellen. Man hat uns dann hinauskomplimentiert und nur mit Mühe bekamen wir unser Geld wieder.
Glücklicher Weise gibt es in dieser Gegend schönere Alternativen !

Noosa North Shores is a lovely natural space and huge.  Although they have a couple of bbq areas there aren’t any camp kitchens on site. Communication from reception can be limited too, during our stay the power was turned off for a scheduled upgrade for over 5 hours without any notifications.
Always love our stay at Noosa North Shore !
Lovely glamoing tents. Very clean and comfortable. A TV and cook burner outside would be good.  Also hair dryer and shampoo/conditioner in the shower
Vi valde platsen för att den hade pub och restaurang. Tyvärr var dessa stängda. Endast öppna på helgerna. Campingen trist, men fina toaletter. Känguru på platsen.

Glamping tents are very nice.  Well appointed with great bathroom & kitchenette has what you need.  Bring a little bbq or gas cooker if you don’t want to use the camp kitchen BBQ’s, although they are close.
During school terms if there is a camp on you can’t use all the facilities (some pools, laser tag, putt putt, indoor bouldering wall etc).  We didn’t know this until arriving, but the management team were great about this and offered great customer service by providing complimentary use for our group once the school had left
We’ll be back

Je recommande vivement ! J’ai passé un séjour incroyable dans un cadre paisible et naturel, entouré de nombreux kangourous. Le camping est d’une propreté irréprochable, que ce soit les sanitaires, l’espace barbecue ou les piscines, qui sont également très bien entretenues. Il propose une variété d’activités, dont le paddle. Nous en avons profité pour en faire au coucher du soleil sur le lac, un moment absolument magique. La mer est à deux pas, offrant ainsi un parfait équilibre entre nature et océan. Tout est réuni pour des vacances inoubliables.
We loved our time at NNSR! The glamping tents are beautiful, loved the location and being amongst the bush and the kids loved the pool, jumping pillow, laser tag etc!
Sehr natürliche Umgebung, große Stellplätze, reinste!!! Amenities, ruhiger Campingplatz. Sehr zu empfehlen für Naturliebhaber... die Kängurus warten auf euch?

Having lived on the Sunshine Coast for 40 years, this was the first time staying at Noosa Northshore Retreat.
I definitely will be coming back.
The grandkids were fully occupied with putt putt, swimming, bike riding etc. Sreen time was completely forgotten.
Great spot to use as a base camp for those that love to go beach fishing.

We had a family weekend away at Noosa North Shores Big 4
Right from when we checked in we felt at home. Our cottage was nice and close to the pool which was great we had an awesome gazebo where you could catch a lovely breeze. The cottage was well presented and felt nice and homely. There was heaps of activities to keep the grandkids busy. I am sure we will be back at some point!!

My husband and I recently stayed at Noosa North Shore in one of their glamping tents.
WOW to say we were blown away is an understatement.
We did not arrive until 7pm and upon entering our tent were immediately impressed firstly by the size and secondly by the styling and attention to detail. (Loved the fairy lights).
On the 2nd night after spending the day 4WDriving on the beach we enjoyed a lovely meal and a couple of cocktails in the restaurant and again we could not find fault with anything.
We will most definitely be back.
Thank you
Great relaxing place.  Lovely walks
The glamping tents are awesome 👍🏻👍🏻 they are pretty new and looking so nice. You feel very comfortable in the middle of the wood with a lot of animals, especially all the Kangaroos. So great!

Nous avons passé un séjour formidable ! Les chambres sont propres et bien aménagées. Nous étions dans le cottage 2 et la vue du coucher de soleil sur le lac est magnifique ! L'ambiance est très calme et relaxante, avec des kangourous tout autour de nous.
Nous étions avec 2 enfants et ils ont adoré leur séjour grâce à toutes les activités proposées.

Our stay in the cottage 2 bedrooms was absolutely wonderful! The cozy atmosphere, clean facilities and the view on the lake made it the perfect holidays. The cottage was well-equipped, and the peaceful setting allowed us to fully relax and enjoy our time. The bar and restaurant serves great food and coffees. Lots of activities around for the kids (jumping pillow, bouldering, pool, laser tag...).
I would definitely recommand!

Beautiful spot to camp. We had an en-suite site with power for our tent, gazebo and gazebo pod. Beach down the road, fishing, great amenities/laundry. Only thing is to take drinking water or buy at the shop at reception. Although there is water, it is not clear water, looks dirty,  so take extra water if you are not wanting to drink water that isn’t clear. Loved our long weekend and dog friendly too.

Great place to camp with plenty of shady campsites. Heaps of fun for kids of all ages with pools, jumping pillow, games room, laser tag, indoor bouldering and mini golf. Lovely clean amenities in the camping area. Easy access to 4WDing on the beach (with a valid permit). Highly recommend this for a family holiday.

None existing kitchen facilities in the whole camping you have 3 small sinks and 6 BBQ burners . No fridge no benches, no cooking facilities. The power camping grounds are in front of the entrance of the camping ground!!! Non-stop noise. Also camping areas are not well maintained .
No kids facilities ! And to top it off, super expensive!!!
Only positive is location and good condition bathrooms and showers.
Pool is also decent.

Excelente lugar para pasar un fin de semana largo! Muy cerca de la playa, muy tranquilo rodeado de naturaleza. El sábado por la noche cenamos en el pub, sencillo y delicioso, todo lo que se necesita, sumado a la amabilidad de quienes trabajan lo hace un lugar para recomendar!
Sin duda volveremos!

It was a very disappointing experience.|The unit were we stay need to  be refreshed and renovated. Old an a bit run down.|Some of the bed linen was stained. |The water was off twice and for the next few day all brown.|The washing machines in the cottage area  were old and dirty.|For the amount of money we paid, it was very disappointing.

Really enjoyed our stay on the non powered site. Very relaxing and everything is so convenient. The staff were extremely helpful and friendly. Best customer service. Also loved that we had no phone service and was an eye opener for the children. There was also a phone booth that was in working order

We stayed here at the Big4 Noosa for 4 nights, we enjoyed our stay and crossed the ferry to see the fireworks as we wouldn't see the from the island but overall enjoyed the holiday park and would go back again just be more prepared with young children and not a car that can go on sand.

Cons - isolating island, jellyfish on beach, ferry fees to cross each time and the holiday park had no water for a night while we were there which mean't we couldn't order food or anything due to no water.

New ownership of caravan park by Big4. Still a lot of improvements to do. The phone reception is poor in the caravan park which makes it very hard to contact reception with any issues. The unpowered sites need to be sun friendly for solar obviously. Saw a lot of workers going to and from work at the bar/shop but none maintaining the grounds of rubbish, gardening etc.

This place is absolutely brilliant, beautiful and welcoming. Staff are amazing from the beginning to end. All the kids activities were run by amazing, energetic staff.

We camped and it was a perfect site. Amenities are great. They had a sign saying they were upgrading which was evident because one shower block was new and one sort of old but still absolutely useable, clean and accessible.

Noosa North Shore as an area was a gift. We didn't do a lot of research when booking but were overjoyed when we discovered how beautiful it was.

For 4WDers, the access to washing facilities is a huge bonus.

Pump tracks were a huge hit with the kids.
Excellent service from the reception desk.  Our request for a fan in the second bedroom was handled promptly.

The “deluxe” sites were TINY. |There’s one location with showers/toilets and a second with toilets only. Big walk to use a bathroom.|The camp “kitchens” have hot plate BBQs from your local park. No kettle, microwave, fridge, oven, nothing.

Our “deluxe” camp site was tiny, I’m honestly baffled how they even considered that space would be suitable or enjoyable for people.

Considering everyone is packed in like sardines, you would expect more than one bathroom block and one block of toilets. Don’t leave going to the last minute, you’re likely miles from a toilet.

Worst of all, the camp “kitchens”. They are hot plate BBQs like you find free at your local park. No fridge, stove, oven, kettle, microwave, boiling water, nothing.

I’m actually amazed that Big 4 would put their name to this, given how good many destinations of theirs are. We’ve camped recently many places between Newcastle and Cairns, this is by far the worst. We won’t be back.

I had a fantastic time celebrating my birthday at BIG4 Noosa Northshore. It was incredible waking up to Kangaroos each morning and enjoying all of the activities the resort had to offer. Fun for families and all ages

Overall, not too bad.  Rustic camp ground that will get better with more attention.  Unfortunately our Deluxe site (incl Water and Power) didn't have any water for 2 of 6 days, and the main pool was out of action for an entire day while they cleaned it.  Bit poor.
Could have more things for kids to do at the retreat side, rather than walking 700 metres to the reception / pub side.  Not bad though

Stayed in a 2 bedroom cabin.
place was sufficient for us.

Food was great, staff were friendly and helpful.
Good for kids.

Lovely place to stay. Really enjoyed our camping trip.
Resort staff in the reception, bar and restaurant were awesome. Always a smile on their faces and so willing to help with anything. Facilities clean and looked after.
We stay here often and love it. The staff are lovely, the place is clean, close to DI and just a fantastic place for everyone to stay. ❤️

The worst campsite ever experienced!  We stay with big4 a lot. This has to be the worse!  The deluxe campsite was tiny!!!!  Right on top of everyone!  The bins were always full of flies and not emptied, no dumb points either!!!!  The swimming pools were both disgusting… no lighting for pool in evening. The whole place is a disgrace. Do not stay here

This is our first time with BIG 4 and won’t be back any BIG4 experience at all. Really disappointed. We booked glamping room 133 during 26-29 December. On the last night happened to be power outage on site. Lost power from 7pm to 1:30am in the morning. It’s freaking hot in the room. Nothing working. Kids screaming and sweating all night due to the heat. Checked out in the morning. Male coffee making reception said “ it’s not our fault “. With attitude. Forgot to get his name. Well I guess then its our own fault to booked with you guys at busy time and realize such BIG 4 company doesn’t have their own power supply incase something went wrong like this. And I guess it’s our own fault to paid over $500 per night cooked in the room due to this big company doesn’t have their own power apply. What a joke!!!!

Never seen a swimming pool green like that. Not maintenance at all. And public BBQ area the hot plate half of it not heated properly.

Only thing I would recommend is the room renovation fresh and clean.
Fantastic stay at a great park!

We had a great stay at BIG4 Noosa North Shore! Being able to bring our dog and stay in one of their dog-friendly glamping tents was such a treat. The tent had everything we needed, including a kitchenette, bathroom, comfy bed and air con. The glamping tent is perfect for nature lovers who prefer a bit more luxury than traditional camping. One of the highlights was renting bikes and SUPs. It’s a great spot for families with young kids and anyone looking for a relaxing getaway.
The Glamping Tents are fantastic, location is excellent and the staff are very attentive. Highly recommend.
It’s is a great quiet place with a lot of wildlife.

Such a peaceful and beautiful place, perfect for reconnecting with nature. I really appreciated how clean and well-maintained everything was. You can tell the staff puts a lot of care into keeping the place in great condition ✨ I felt so comfortable here and will definitely be coming back! 🍃
Great access to beaches

Noosa North Shore retreat, is that unique place where you can experience a beautiful Bushland setting with wildlife surrounding you.

The site has many things to do for both young and old.

We use it for our annual Karate camp and have for nearly 20 years.

It started so well, we were loving it. Booked for three nights we were excited to spread the activities out. It turns out, bouldering and laser tag aren’t open Sunday… so there goes that (note: apparently laser tag is only open for an hour and a half one day… you can’t just go whenever you like with friends).
