The adventure high ropes advertised aren’t open either. No where is this advertised - not on the website and not upon check-in. You’d think upon check in there would be a run down of facilities/activities, when and how to book them etc. It would have been nice to know this to plan our stay around open times. Unfortunately it’s left a sour taste about the park, which otherwise is lovely.

We have been coming to Noosa North Shore Retreat for about 4 years and it's great to see how the property is continuously improved in facilities and services.
Aaron Jensen - F&B Manager is always super helpful and friendly with our group and all the visitors at the property.
Highly recommend  this place to come over with friends or family and stay in a cabin which are great size for a fun weekend away!

Attended music event, Ozfest. What an outstanding location. Set right in nature. So beautiful. Staff and service was excellent.
Music was fabulous. Such a great event at an amazing spot. Couldn’t be better. Thank you

My biggest disappointed during our stay at BIG4 Noosa North Shore was the staff.  From my experience the customer was not their #1 priority - more interested in socialising or looking at their mobile phones.  The campground is open and well-grassed but amentities were limited for size of campground - one amenity block with 4 showers and 4 toilets and one with 3 toilets.  Most activities are located around the reception some distance from the camground.  I have stayed at many BIG4 parks and have found staff to be always welcoming and very helpful.  Unfortunately, I can not say the same for BIG4 Noosa North Shore.

Awesome glamping stay at BIG4 Noosa North Shore. The tent was so comfortable, spacious and beautifully decked out.
The facilities are great and the park is beautiful.

We held a fundraising festival for our charity and everything was amazing from organising the event to post event and bumping out. The kids had the best time which allowed all the parents to sit back and enjoy the music. We will definitely use the venue again and hopefully regularly
Went on a camping weekend fantastic venue , clean amenities and close to the swimming pool.

Beautiful place to be. The beach is near by and so is an amazing nature walk. Staff is always there when you need them, absolutely recommend go on vacation here!
Holiday mode on;)
Our 1st time in a glamping tent and it didn’t disappoint. Very spacious and beautifully styled accommodation. Nature at our doorstep.  It was a truly relaxing 4 night stay!

I really enjoyed my stay here. Waking up surrounded by beautiful trees,  kangaroos,  A beautiful beach and lake nearby. The onsite restaurant and cafe was good. I enjoyed breakfast and dinner  there. The room was comfortable.The pool was clean and just loved the location was amazing.
It was hard to leave. Very enjoyable experience.

Spending 2 nights on the campsite and we hoped for some more, big4 camping has been great for us as a family but this place wasn’t really that good. Camp kitchen really need an upgrade and playground, jumping pillow and other activities for kids are fare away from the campsite. Our kids wanted to play mini golf but since there was a school trip, it wasn’t possible even though they didn’t play - seems a bit weird that we pay for a campsite with kids friendly activities but only are allowed to use sometimes when it fits… we are definitely disappointed.
All fabulous

We stayed at the campsite over the weekend for a mini break. The atmosphere and location are beautiful, however we were put on a VERY tight site that was cramped in between other campers where we had little place to park and felt like we were on top of our neighbours. We had also arranged a late check out but someone ended up being put on our site earlier than late check out time when there were lots of other empty sites nearby. We also didn't like the distance from the camping ground to the main pub and restaurant area. There really needs to be a small bar/cafe at the campsite so you don't have to drive to the main area (it was too far to walk with kids at night). You also needed to drive to the beach and to the lake which to us defeated the purpose of being in nature. Whilst we enjoyed the mini break I don't know if we will stay again and if we do, it wouldn't be for very long (max 4 nights).
This was our 3rd stay. We love it. And the pool was incredible and so refreshing. Our TV was not working and it was replaced straightaway. Management are amazing.

We stayed 2 nights in glamping accommodation and can’t recommend it highly enough!
Our family of 4 were blown away with the quality of accommodation, service and activities.
So much for everyone to do and stay active.
Put this one to the top of your must do list!!

Lovely camping spot with family and friends. Good variety of grassy flat spots to choose from. Terrific modern/clean bathroom/laundry facilities. Staff at desk and bar/restaurant were all very friendly and provided excellent service

We recently had a 2-night stay at the Glamping tent, and it was perfect. Clean, tidy, and spacious. The kids had a lot of fun exploring the park and using the facilities as well. Highly recommend it, and we will be back for sure! Perfect

We were a tad anxious about our stay after reading some negitive reviews about the standards of the facilities and the rudness of reception staff.
Reception staff were extremely friendly and helpful and the facilities had recently been upgraded, so the shower block is modern, clean and the paper towel and toilet paper replenished regularly.
We were 4 families camping. We were positioned next to the dam in a quiet spot.
There is a pool, however we did not use this as we went to the beach instead but it looked spotless.
Sites were clean and well maintained. Option to bring your own portable camp fire was great too. Lots of birds and kangaroos which the kids loved too. Would recommend and return 👌

We absolutely loved our stay at Noosa North Shore Retreat! I organised a camping trip with most of the families in my son’s school year (7 and 8 year olds). About 130 of us stayed at the retreat in both cottages & glamping. All the staff at NNSR were friendly, helpful and organised. Our accomodation in the cottages & glamping was lovely, clean, comfortable & modern. The kids had the absolute best fun! NNSR organised a fabulous group laser tag + dinner event for 65 kids and the kids are still raving about it. The food at the bistro is really fresh and delicious too! The kids loved the bouncing cushion, the two outdoor pools, the bouldering wall, the mini golf and the proximity to the most beautiful beaches in the Sunshine Coast. Highly, highly recommend NNSR!!
Great location with clean accommodation and amenities

The camping site was to loud with music and people too much drunk… we werw at site 143 and our next neighbours stop the music at midnight so kids and wife couldn’t get to sleep.
Our experience wasn’t the best, there is no kitchen to cook basic meals for kids.
I hope they improve their structure soon.

Wecamped at Noosa North Shore Retreat and we were very pleased with the size of the sites and the cleanliness of the ablution facilities. The staff were friendly and knowledgeable andwe will definitely return for another camp at Noosa North Shore Retreat.

The rooms are in a reasonable quality for a holiday park. The services, activities and location is perfect. Great place for any kind of person with multiple activities available. Great access to the lake and beach. The reception gave us a call before arrival to double check the arrival time and if an afterhours check in is required which was a Suprise to me and much appreciated.

The place is lovely and kid-friendly.
Too many activities to tell which pre-occupies the little ones.

I recently stayed at this camping site and unfortunately had a disappointing experience. First, the lack of a fridge made food storage a real challenge, especially in warm weather. The fees and charges were quite steep for what was offered, which felt unfair given the amenities. The kitchen facilities were not well-maintained; they were lacking in basic supplies and cleanliness. Additionally, the ground was not compacted, making it uncomfortable to set up tents and difficult to navigate. Overall, I wouldn’t recommend this site unless significant improvements are made.

We stayed in the glamping tent and the tent itself was really nice, it was beautiful, although it was dusty and I ended up buying antihistamines because I couldn't stop sneezing. Not that this is a fault of the venue but i will add mosquitoes are really bad, ive never seen them that bad anywhere and we teavelled australia full time, they were extreme!!!!The 2 male managers were amazing, couldn't fault them, however that's where my positive review ends....I was so disappointed that my kids were not able to do anything..we were here for 2 nights and the days we were there the activities my son wanted to do had closed, there was no instructions or itinerary stating the ages kids could do things and times/days so it was all a guessing game and when you asked a staff member they were clueless. No signs pointing to where things were, yes you were given a map but you only get a map of the half of the park you're staying in not the full park and it's  very confusing, my son was able to go for a swim and do the climbing wall that was it...mini golf wasn't worth it, was messy and outdated and you have to pay extra for all your activities, they do bike hire and when I asked I was told they hire out bikes for kids, anyway, got there and went to hire one for my 7 year old to do the bike track to find out the extra small bike "for a child" was almost too big for myself as an adult!!!! It was huge, my son is a big 7 year old (hes tall) and he could not get on and off the bike it was ridiculous. The icing on the cake for me was when I asked for 2 hot chocolates at reception, I specifically stated they were for 2 children and to please have them Luke warm. When I got them I was carrying them to the table and my hand was getting hot so I tried it before giving it to the kids and it scolded my tongue it was hotter than my coffee. I went back and asked the lady nicely if I could have a replacement, her exact words to me were "oh wow you must be very sensitive because that's not hot at all" and said it while smirking at me like I was an idiot. I said to her "excuse me no it is incredibly hot, try it yourself.if you don't believe me" she wouldn't try it. She then proceeded to pour one of the hot cups into 2 new cups them filled them up with milk, so basically it was now a cup of milk....she looked at me and said "happy?" I couldn't not believe what was happening, another customer standing next to me said "omg that's disgusting behaviour I'd be putting in a complaint" that's how bad it was other people around me were angry at the way she was treating me, I said "no I'm not happy I'd like 2 new Luke warm drinks like I asked for" and threw the cups into the sink aggressively and said "fine if that's what you insist on" I was upset at this point and said "actually forget it, I want a refund and I want to speak to your boss" she told me "no you can't speak to him he's busy" I had to ask 3 times before she finally agreed to get someone for me, she was refusing to let me speak to her boss, I then asked for her first name so I knew who to complain about and she refused to tell me and her response when I asked was "okay sweetheart what's your name then? If you want to me know mine" she was so nasty, rude and condecening, again after I pushed the matter she finally told me her name was AMBER, she is the blonde lady working in reception, older, in her late 40's-50's, austrlaian accent. Her behaviour just absolutely put the icing on the cake for me, I will never come back because of the way she treated me, I will add the manage, I believe his name was Adam or Arron, I can't remember!! But he was nice, he gave me a refund and re made the drinks for us and offered to get some food for my kids, I was happy with his response, he is a great manager and should be praised :) I was very happy with him and the other male manager, he was so upbeat and friendly and amazing at customer service!!! So thank you to the both of them as they made me feel much better about my stay.
spacious camp sites, excellent customer service, quite close to the beach

We stayed in the glamping accommodation for 7 days and definitely recommend! We are a family of four with a 6 year old and 4 year old and we were very comfortable. The ensuite was big and the beds were comfortable.
The styling was gorgeous and in touch with the area. We would have liked to have had a hot plate or personal bbq to use but we brought our own weber and did our cooking that way instead of using the bbq facilities. Our friends stayed at a powered slab site not far from us so we could experience both options but go back to a comfortable bed especially when the winds and rain came! Highly recommend!

I stayed at the campground. Great space with newly renovated facilities. I went up to the main reception where the staff were very friendly and helpful. The food and drinks at the restaurant and bar were good and well priced. They had heaps of things to keep the kids busy. It is close to the beach and 4WD access tracks. I would definitely recommend and go back to.

The room was clean and comfortable. However hot water runs out before all guests finished shower.
Need to take a ferry to access the site, which cost $15 one way.
Kids attended school holiday program these holidays and had an absolute blast and have had so much fun. They want to go back again

Our grandchildren have just enjoyed fun activity filled days with the school holiday program. They have enjoyed Laser tag, Archery and archery tag, bouldering and beach games. They are excited for tomorrow as high ropes is on the scheduled along with radio rogaine

We camped at the retreat this weekend. Loved it. Amenities clean and well maintained. The showers were exceptional.. very very nice.

Will stay again. Lots to do.

Loved Dogzilla. He came and said hi and gave us lots of cuddles. Didn’t want to tell him to go home 🤣

Friendly and genuine staff to make you feel welcome..perfect location and activities galore.. this place is a hidden gem northern Sunshine Coast..we’ll be back for sure and will bring more friends too!
Beautiful spacious glamping tent in great spot. Would highly recommend.

Great cabin and outdoor area. It's quiet and perfect for kids to run around and have fun.
We'll come back!!!!!

We loved our stay here over the weekend, the place is perfect for families. The cottages are a little outdated however they’re well laid out and have everything you need. There’s so many fun activities for the kids although we do wish the mini golf was included in the price of your stay. The food from the tavern was delicious and such a good spot to eat while the kids play. It’s such a beautiful spot with so much space and amazing wildlife and overall we really enjoyed our stay and the kids had a ball!

I would give it a 3.5 star. We stayed in an ensuite site which was good, spacious and clean. The pool was clean but a little outdated. We went there with a toddler and there wasn't much for them to do. We went to the main reception area which was either a drive or a 500m walk (not conveniently close) and the playground was also outdated. There’s the new bouldering area but you had to pay per person to use it! So we opted not to do it. Just doesn't compare to other Big4. Hopefully that will change. Both days we ended going back to the mainland as there was more to see and do as we dont have a 4x4 however we spent $100 just in ferry fee. Don’t think we’ll be back in the near future as it just didn’t suit our family. Staff were friendly and helpful.

Prachtige locatie en een heel groot park die pas sinds juli 2024 een big4 is. Ze hebben allerlei activiteiten die je kunt doen. De service was top en ze waren zeer behulpzaam! Het enige waar je rekening mee moet houden is dat er door de weeks heel veel schoolkinderen zijn en dan kunnen de activiteiten niet worden gedaan. Het is echter een prachtige omgeving en daar is genoeg te doen.
Clamping tents where great bit off both worlds, no tv was a bonus good for family time. Location is amazing, only downfall we found was food wasn't the best lacking variety

Terrible experience. We opened the door to the room and were met by an overpowering smell of stale cigarettes. We tried to put our food in the fridge only to see cockroaches in the fridge (alive). The toilet was filthy, the shower had mold everywhere and no door, which had clearly been removed. The bathroom sink didn’t drain. When we tried to drink the water we couldn’t as it was yellow. One of our party did drink the water and got sick with D&V. Then there was the incessant drumming from the tribal drum group that had booked some time there.  The drumming started at 10am and went to 9:30pm.  We raised the matter of the noise only to be told that the group would be leaving soon, they did not. The raised the matter of the water and the filthy bathroom. On the water matter they said it was from the dam and filtered. When asked directly if it was safe to drink we were just told it was filtered but need another filter. (Bring bottled water). With the bathroom we were told that we were lucky as that room had one of the better bathrooms and that they would be renovated one day. I would hate to see the other bathrooms if ours was one of the better ones. We will never recommend this place or ever go there again.

Great park to stay at.
We had a grassy, shaded powered site, well signed and lots of space.
Short walk to the pub for dinner and a short drive to the beach.

We had a wonderful stay at Noosa North Shore at the weekend! We stayed in site 122, the ensuite was ideal for us as a family. There is plenty to do at the resort and all the staff were super friendly! The food was nice and we were given a late checkout on Sunday which allowed us time to explore the beaches.||We will be back for a third visit!
1 off the staff can come across very abrupt

We booked two nights here last minute in our motorhome and stupidly didn't read reviews beforehand as it's basically a big kiddie park with kids running around screaming, most of the time and it wasn't school holidays or the weekend. Great if your groups of families travelling together, but if your a couple after peace and quiet, avoid.
The top area toilets and showers are really good, but the toilet block next to the lower area is in dire need of upgrading.
Owners posted a reply to this saying it was organised groups of kids on the oval but that's incorrect as we didn't see any groups of kids on the oval next to our campsite just campers kids running amuck without supervision as mentioned.noisy!
Good ammenities a wonderful place to have a holiday
Service was great but for camp site water and power was great but ground was very wet under foot

We arrived and checked into reception. We proceeded to ask about the activities and when we could book them, the lady at reception said we weren't allowed to use any of them as the school group were arriving the next day. We had brought both our kids bikes as it was his birthday and was super excited about the mountain bike track. Nothing was mentioned on the website about the activities being closed for school groups and we wouldn't have come if this was the case. The Lazer tag also was only available 2-3 Saturday arvo when we check out. We paid alot of money for the glamping site and assumed the facilities would be open to all guests. The way the reception handled it also was unprofessional and rude and said "put it in an email", which we did and heard nothing back. Unprofessional management and clearly don't care about their guests. Won't be back!

Had a wonderful time celebrating my sons wedding at your lovely resort. All staff were particularly helpful and friendly and helped make our stay awesome. The food was amazing and I hope to visit again soon. Thanks for a great time had by us all

didnt know about the expensive ferry service until the day of

room was very plain - tv was super tiny

it was extremely basic like a roadside motel

if you have kids it may be alright - they have a bouncing pillow and pool - but be prepared to pay to cross the ferry twice every time you want to visit town which gets expensive fast - for noosa it is cheap but there is a reason for that...

Secluded spot perfect for a get away and rest.  Probably should have an option of free travel on barge and your Big4 park would get more visitors as this adds to the cost quite considerably.  We enjoyed our time here and dinner Friday night was fantastic. New amenities are great 👍 Customer service excellent.

What a lovely place the staff that work there are fantastic.
We went to my daughter's wedding on the weekend which Noosa North Shore Retreat hosted.Aaron the manager is amazing.
The staff that was working on the day/ night was the best.
Thankyou for our wonderful stay

Thankyou for welcoming us and making our Nieces wedding a very memorable occasion. The service, the food the location was perfect. Fun time was had by all. Plenty a space for the kids to play and burn up some energy.
Fantastic place for families or a romantic getaway. The staff are friendly, accommodating, and professional. Great food, great atmosphere, and great service. Thank you

A Nice campsite however  a children's playground at the campsite would be nice, also no camp kitchen is pretty poor considering the price you pay, and I don't understand why they say no fires yet will rent you a fire pit for $30.

The website indicated that there was a lot to do for children. In reality it was not available during weekdays in July. The pool area was also not quite as we had expected. WiFi was only available at the reception, which is minimal 500 meter walk away and closed end of the afternoon.We left a day earlier for things to do. The site is really quiet at night and not far from the ocean.

My family of five and I recently enjoyed a two night stay in the new glamping tents at Noosa Northshore. They were absolutely amazing.  Well thought out. Lots of space. Very clean and tidy. A luxury feel. The whole camp ground is fantastic with all of the activities and we loved being able to go driving on the beach in our 4wd.

We took our caravan and It is a nice park set in the National forest with power but no water. Bathroom facilities are brand new and lovely. Camp kitchen is very basic, bbq only. The playground and jumping pillow is back at reception which is approx 1km from the campgrounds so it's not very convenient when you have little kids. A playground for little kids on the campground would be great.  It is nice but a bit expensive. Also I think it should be mentioned clearly on the website that the only way to get here is by Ferry which happens to be very expensive! I booked this location because it was a Big4 and we just needed a one night stopover, not knowing we needed to catch a Ferry as well which cost $30 each way. We would come back but again but next time for a longer stay.
Great location. Sites are very generous, never felt over crowded. Definitely a return location for some beach and sand driving.

We've just returned from a 6 night stay here. We absolutely loved it - we desperately needed a holiday from work, and we were able to stop, rest and recuperate.
Our cabin was lovely, newly painted, and had most practical things we needed. As we were going to be there for a week, we brought plenty of food, and a slow cooker, which was the best decision. Our cabin looked down to the lake, and we enjoyed watching a school's rowing cohort walk back and forth most days. The sunset was lovely over the water too.
There was plenty to do for our kids: 10 and 12 years. We ended up dropping our eldest home (locally) as he needed wi-fi for his Yr 12 assignments, and it wasn't available for him. Great for us, not for him - but it all worked out well. We enjoyed lovely walks through the forest, the kids rode their bikes (when it wasn't raining), and loved the mountain bike track, and even got a game of tennis in, in between rain showers.
We loved sitting on the sofa with our books and hot chocolate - reading for hours.
The staff were delightful, and helped us out with anything we needed.
The pub and restaurant was lovely - the food was excellent.
We look forward to returning in better weather so that we can also enjoy the pools.
A great stay for short and longer breaks! Thank you all.

We absolutely loved it here. All staff are very friendly, the cabins are nicely spaced out so you have your own little private area. In a great location with lovely walks and lots of things for the kids to do. Our son absolutely loved the rock climbing such a great idea! We can’t wait to come back in the summer time and enjoy the pools and beach etc. we went to the restaurant both nights for dinner and the food was outstanding and great pricing.

This is a great park for a bush campground experience and is good for large groups.

The grounds are beautiful and it’s very peaceful. There isn’t much phone service so a great place to disconnect.

It’s a large park - From the powered caravan sites we were on it’s about a 500-700m walk or bike ride through a trail back to the main venue where there is a bike track, laser tag, climbing room, mini golf and a bar/restaurant. We had our own bikes but I believe you can hire some too

Campfires are permitted but you’ll need to bring your own fire pit. You can buy firewood from the shop on site.

They have a great activity list for kids aged 9-12, which unfortunately didn’t suit our young family. The fenced playground near the restaurant was a nice area.

This park is not close to town if you want to come and go as you have to take the North Shore Ferry to get here - $12 one way for a large vehicle (RAM), $24 towing our caravan. We made one trip into town and ended up almost $80 in ferry fees.

It’s a short drive to the beach if you want to 4WD on the beach. Jump online to get your permit, $15 for a day.

The campsites were spacious. We have a large caravan and our campsite on Python Place. One downside was we had no access to grey water, there’s a ‘canal’ on the top side of our site but water doesn’t run uphill so it wasn’t an option for us to run our pipe there and although it hadn’t rained we found the ground damp underfoot and a bit muddy.

There is no dump point in the park but a 5 minute drive up to cutting 2 there are several points you can use.

The main amenities block is brand new, clean and very nice.

Didn't use the rooms had my camper but they look great. This park is one of if not the best park I've ever stayed in . Plenty of room for the kids to run around and the activities are excellent highly recommend big 4 Noosa north shore

I had my camper trailer and I had a lovely sunny location as it is in the middle of winter. The shower and toilet block has just been newly renivated and in looks amazing. Showers are great!! And also everything is so clean and well looked after.
If you are planning a visit be sure to take some apples with you because the lorikeets love them and will fly from the trees directly to your arms or head. They are so tame. An excelent experience.

We had a lovely time in the caravan park, so I haven't rated the service and rooms. A lovely big pitch with lots of wildlife. We also enjoyed the fire pit, cafe, pub and mini golf. A bit of everything. Very special. Thank you.

We stayed over the recent long weekend, so peaceful, if you want to enjoy nature and the beach this is the place to stay, no high rises no gaudy shops , direct access onto the beach by 4wd. Kilometers of beautiful beach to park and fish or swim in amazing clear water. Then just a short drive through bush track to rainbow beach with awesome lagoons to sup or kayak on. Yes you have to cross by the ferry so I'd make sure you pack everything you need to buy with you , mossie spray etc, but the small convenience shop does have things you might have forgotten. The restaurant on site is great well priced, and the activities for kids jumping pillow mini golf etc is enough to keep everyone busy , bring bikes as there is so much area for the kids to ride on. A great family holiday or getaway with friends so much better then traffic and shops in the middle of Noosa. Will stay again now that we have found it.

My wife and I and our four children recently camped here and cannot fault the place.
The campsite was clean and easy to access and find. The amenities blocks were some of the best we have seen.
We had a rainy weekend and that still didn’t dampen our stay with so many activities to keep the whole family busy.
The kids especially loved the indoor bouldering and mountain bike track!
Another highlight was interacting with the wildlife- rainbow lorikeets and kangaroo spotting.
We enjoyed dinner at the onsite restaurant and the service and food were both wonderful.
Highly recommend coming here.

We had a wonderful time at Noosa North Shore, The staff were amazing especially when I made an error on what I had booked. The food at the restaurant was delicious and plentiful. The kids loved all the attractions and the local beach was awesome to drive on and also fish from. I recommend to anyone! Highlight for us was the late afternoons seeing the kangaroos hopping through the campgrounds.

Great location, nice and excellent facilities.
Would recommend to anyone looking for a quite spot near Noosa
Will definitely be back!

Great place to park up and explore
New and modern  amenities and the reception /bar/ restaurant was sooo welcoming

We arrived here at 6:00pm cause Noosa North Beach Campground was full booked and they mentioned Noosa North Shore Retreat may have available spots for camping. Lucky us they did and we loved it!!! Nice areas and great showers. We would definitely come back! Btw, bring cash cause the ferry to cross is 20$ return cash only.

We enjoyed our stay at the camping. It was a  beautiful and peaceful place. The sanitary are very clean and new.
People and staff are super nice !
The pub is also very good.
Perfect place to see kangourous and a lot of birds. We recommended !
Great spot and good management, thanks

While packing up our camping spot to come home found that we had been on an Ants Nest and they had gone through our camp and our Waeco fridge.
We’re told we couldn’t have a fire there yet we saw several fires were being had,having a fire is part of the camping experience

The cabins are very dated and feel like they are abandoned, the grounds around the retreat could definitely use a little TLC to give it a “retreat” feel. Pool wasn’t overly clean with most of the pool chairs mouldy. There was also no reception nor any wifi in the cabins but was at the pub. The staff were lovely but the menu could use more options. Had the steak for dinner with three other people at our table and we all got different size/cuts of meat with only one steak option on the menu.

We camped for 2 weeks. Noosa North Shore Retreat is so tranquil with lots of trees. Sitting back watching the kangaroos, birds and goanna's pass by was a hit with the kids. So close to go to the beach or a swim at the pool, a short walk to the retreat pub for dinner. Very clean and new amenities! We didn't want to leave definitely recommend to all.

Got a deluxe power and water site spot 63 had ankle deep mud and water. Was told by staff that it does flood there. So don't accept a spot from 60 to 68 it just drains straight to these sites. Bathrooms were clean and beautiful pool was amazing overall a beautiful place we were put in a flood spot
Had a lovely experience. Staff friendly. Bathrooms tidy and clean.
Rooms is not applicable as we camped at a non powered grass spot. Was great though, good view, kangaroos and toilets / showers were very clean.

We had such a great time camping and so did the 3 other families travelling with us. The bathrooms are very new and super clean. The kids and adults alike had a ball at laser skirmish & mini golf. Great place to bring the bikes. It was close to the beach access track to head up to double island point. The kids loved having the lorikeets on the arms and heads. Lots of kangaroos & ducks about too. The staff were all very friendly and accommodating.

Not even the rain could dampen spirits during our 3 day stay at this park. Whether you’re into laying by the pool, tackling the bike trials, getting competitive with the kids or pals on the high ropes or Lazer tag - there’s something for everyone here.
The park is located in a little piece of paradise and has a pub/cafe on site that serves great food and drinks. And amazing staff to boot! Thanks Aaron, for being a superstar legend! 🙏🏾

Lovely camp site. Lots of activities and kids programs and 4WD access to beach.|Gemma at Reception is extremely helpful and an asset on their staff base.|A communal kitchen/fridge/microwave at Camp site will help......................

Great park for the kids lots of activities to keep them busy. Pool, laser tag, putt putt, bouldering, arcade games, bmx track. Love the new toilets and shower block. Only 1.5kms to the beach. Lots of natural wild life around. Definitely will be back in the future.

4 powered and water sites were booked together and all sites 83-86 were muddy. Sites didn’t have grey water. Buns were far 200m away. Worst experience I’ve ever camped due to the drainage problem. Many other campers refused to camp near us due to mud which forced them to move on. On the good side the place had a pool but we were to book again it will be at the top near the pool with slab sites. The place needs to fix road as it was slope and pot holes. I haven’t been back over 6 years. The pool also need lighting.

Aaron and the team at the desk where amazing.  After our campsite got washed out they worked hard to find a solution and then proceeded to continue to provide fantastic service for the entire stay.  Great park in a great position and kids had an unbelievably amazing time!
It was a bit wet from all the rain but the staff were very to helpful to make us as comfortable as possible :)

We paid $650 for 2 nights for something they should pay me to stay at.
The "Cottage" Rooms look like if you walked into a house in 2024, from the 80s, that was abandoned during the 70s...dark, dirty, dated and simply horrible.
The carpets were from the 80s and likely never cleaned since then.
The couch, again from the 80s and who knows when it was cleaned..

Service excep for the "kids" working there? was also average at best.
Food again, rough as well...with a Chicken Parmigiana burnt to a crisp...for which I asked...is this how it's supposed to be? To get a "yeah mate"...
Possibly the worst place I've been to in the last 2 years....and that includes Egypt!
DO NOT BOOK THE COTTAGES!
Rent a caravan instead.

Stayed at the camping and clamping ground.  We arrived half an hour early after being told " Our check in time is 1pm. However,  there is a chance you will be able to check in at an earlier time." Another member of our party who arrived the next day was also told the same thing and then told no early checkin.   When we went to check in a very officious male told us to sit and wait and he would check us in after he had checked the resort guests in at 1pm and the "school bus" had left.  We were left with another 5 or so couples to wait for the resort guests to be checked in which he commenced at 1pm. I did ask if he would like me to do paperwork while waiting to be permitted to go to campground but he responded no. He would stick with his processes. This young male may have considered he was being efficient however we and at least two other couples that we talked to considered him just plain rude. We had no explanation for the delay in our checkin, what the relevance of the school bus was,  or why the campground guests (6 couples) were treated as second rate guests.    |Apart from this rude young male the campgrounds were lovely and tidy. We would stay again were we not treated as  second rate guests as we were.

Best destination on the Sunshine Coast, stayed in the new glamping tents. Absolutely awesome and one of our favourite things was listening to the soft rain on a tent without the worry of it leaking.
Perfect little weekend getaway
Rooms a bit outdated but that wasn’t an issue as the location and service was excellent. The tavern makes delicious food and the staff is very friendly. Will definitely come back.

Great place to stay, peaceful location with everything you need. Rooms and the whole park were very well kept and very clean and tidy. No air con in the room but not the end of the world, everything else made up for it.

Been coming here for almost 20 years.  It had been a year since we came last and was really impressed with the recent upgrades to facilities.   Lots of space for the kids and always a nice walk up to the pub for a meal.
Stayed in the family 4 bedroom cabin, very comfortable for a large family would definately recommend!

Really enjoyed our stay on the powered sites, clean amenities, which had been upgraded. Great central location to the beach, and lake. Great coffee and the shop was reasonably priced. Kids enjoyed the pool. Definately will be back soon.

Good spot, some renovating has been done which has improved amenities block. I think power outlet locations need changing as they continue renovating and maybe have some water powered sites closer to pool area as it looks like they are all down the back. Will definitely stay again.

Great Customer Service Received. We booked here last minute and we are disappointed we didn’t stay longer and found this place sooner.  Amazing camp, clean and tidy. Great set up for all different types of accommodation needs. It appears this facility has everything you need on site.

Staff on reception were very friendly and helpful. Credit to the team and facility. We are already making plans on returning in the next few weeks.

Thank you so much for the hospitality.

We visited the Restaurant at the Noosa North Shore Retreat and it's a fantastic restaurant to visit if you have kids and just want to relax. There's a jumping pillow, playground, indoor bouldering, putt putt, laser tag and more...  with peaceful surroundings. Kids can play while the rest enjoy a delicious lunch or drink. We ordered the Lemon Pepper Chicken Wings and Calamari and it was delish. This venue is also great for kids birthday parties 🥳 Only negative is that you have to pay $20 ferry ride if you're just going for lunch so plan to do some other things while you're on the North Shore.
Stayed in a Deluxe Powered Site.  Amenities very clean.  Great facilities.

Loved staying at Noosa North Shore Retreat. The best part about this place is the cute wildlife (saw so many kangaroos) and the beautiful surroundings. So much to do from bushwalks to relaxing on the empty beach and visiting the lake. Really enjoyed our time and will be back soon!
Loved this hidden gem.

I was a little disappointed but then when you remember that they are cabins and not an air bnb it makes sense. It was still a fun place with a toddler. All she needed was the pool. But just beware that all you are getting is shelter and air con. If you want ANYTHING more than bring it. The kitchen is not stocked. There’s no oven ware. There’s no little touches like hand wash or pegs or a comfortable couch. The stove burners were barely hot. I had to use the bbq for the campers to cook. The shower doors don’t close. I would guess it hasn’t been updated since the 90’s.

We stayed in the caravan park and the updates they are doing to park are good. As for the hotel itself it's always nice to chill out in with plenty for the kids to do. We stay for tens days every January and have been for the last 3 years it's the perfect place to set up a bas camp to explore teewah beach and beyond to the lagoon and rainbow Beach. Facilities in the park are ok however with it getting more popular it needs to be cleaned more regularly and a larger laundry is needed.

We stayed for 5 nights in cottage number 8, as soon as we arrived our daughter heard squeaking and scratching inside the wall in her bedroom. There was a mouse in the wall, we don’t think it was able to get out but we would hear it running within the walls and squeaking occasionally. Aaron the site manager was very helpful and attentive sending maintenance over to try sort out the mouse situation however there wasn’t much that could be done. They said they could move us but we would have to leave a night early due to other bookings, so we stayed in the cabin - they gave us live mouse traps however I don’t think it could actually get inside the cabin. The bathroom is outdated and the shower is suited for someone 5 foot, my partner is 6 foot and he was at eye level with the shower head - we both had to duck to get the water over our head. The service at the hotel was average at best - our daughter is coeliac and the website states they can cater to allergies and gluten intolerances however when we asked if they had anything gluten free they had nothing and said the restaurant does but it’s not coeliac safe. The restaurant did not open the whole time we were there despite the website stating it would be open for the dates we were there.  The bistro, reception, convenience store and bar closed at 4 pm every day meaning the only option for dinner was to drive and take the ferry to get food or cook - which is fine however we hadn’t prepared to be cooking every meal as the website stated the restaurant/bistro would be open for dinner.
The pools and resort location was nice, bring bug spray if you stay as mosquitoes are intense - we made the most of our time there however we wouldn’t come back again.
Ps the coffee is also average at best
Great place for a couple of days with the kids, surfing, paddle boarding, 4WD on the beach - perfect aussie getaway!!

We stayed in a lake view cabin.
The room is incredibly old and in desperate need of maintenance.
No shower door, barely working exhaust fan in bathroom, very few utensils for kitchen, no bench space for food prep, no aircon to cabin and fan to main bed (which is also kitchen and living and dining area) barely working so no airflow, fridge gauge broken and not cool enough and water from tap was visibly brownish in color

We stayed for two nights and had plans to attend on-site restaurant however venue decided last minute to close at 4pm due to “minimal guests”
We witnessed many families staying this night.
Plus being on an “island” as such and having to pay to ferry across meant we hadn’t purchased food items to cook. We were informed of closure after returning from noosa.

Pool near location was lovely and large, full of debris however.
This facility has so much potential but feel as though it needs a full refurb and for staff to inform guests of closures prior for preparation

Very disappointing to be honest

Great location, very clean amenities, WIFI boosters would make stay more enjoyable for younger kids other than the pool in the camping area, all other children activities are up at main reception and not close enough for younger children to walk too.
but overall grate spot.

We stayed at the Noosa North Shore Glamping and Camping Park for three weeks over xmas and new year. We had a powered site and luckily we were close to the power box, however others had to run power leads 10 to 15 metres to their caravan. There were no taps to connect hose to for water, no grey water waste drains so every one had to collect water from the four taps in one area for yhe entire large caravan park and everyone just let yhrir grey water run out all over other peoples sites. The caravan was absolutely crowded over xmas new year period and four four days there was no soap in one of the four dispeners in one amenities and after i advised the cleaner she put sanitiser in it as they had obviously run out of hand wash. There were no lights in the laundry and the washers and dryers were card only, no cash. The swimming pool was extremely poorly maintained. I didnt ever see the pool being vacuumed and there was sand, leaves, psoer, stones and other debris on the bottom of the pool. My granddaughter cut her foot rather badly on some sharp concrete that had broken away from the dide of the pool. I never once heard the filter running and it was like a swamp as it was very heavily used all day everyday.  The water was totally cloudy and it was very obvious the chemicals were all wrong. We did not swim in the for pool fir the last two weeks as it was yoo disgustingly dirty and we felt it was highly likely someone would get sick from swimming in it. We staff at the resort were very rude and mostly unhelpful and quite incompetent at their job. The manager and another staff member stood in the doorway chatting on new years eve making it almost impossibe for us customers to get through, we booked a table fir xmas ever for 16 people and then just after we had ordered our food we were told that staff wanted to go home to be with family so we needed to hurry up and finish eating and leave the resturant, some of us had bought bottles of wine which we had to take with us. They did not inform us when we booked that we had yo be put by 8pm. New years eve we had booked a table for 22,  8 months prior. At no time until we arrived did staff tell us the kitchen closed st 7.30 and we all had to leave as soon as we had eaten. We asked if there was entertainment for new years eve. We were told they had a high number of bookings fir dinner so they probably wouldnt bother having live entertainment and they didn't.  We were plsing the games in yhe games room on one occassion and the disc on the air hockey got stuck and wouldnt come out of slot.  I went and told staff and two came over snd just as they got thrir my partner banged the machine and the disc came  out but most of pur time had gone do i asked if they could give us more time and their response was simply to look at us and walk away. We were so very dissapointed with how the staff didnt seem to care about  customers experience and did not care for the facilities making it actually quite risky in terms of getting ill while staying there. They appeared to be more concerned with their own needs and definately did not do anything to encourage customers to return. Menu was limited and expensive as was produce sold in the little shop. One day we were about to walk out of shop, games reception area but had to wait until it was safe to do so as the manager was actually backing his car into the entrance. Sadly we will not be staying at Noosa North Shore Retreat again.

Amazing spot it's a shame about the management. Beautiful location but the staff don't seem to care eg, the pool was filthy during the xmas period they did not keep it clean with the number of people. We could not swim in it. Im surprised people were not getting sick. The pub only serves dinner till 730 and the allow you book a table to buy bottles of wine etc with dinner only to tell you 30min later they are closing. Some of staff are rude at the hotel and reception. And are not interested in creating a place people want to spend time and money they are more interested in minimal effort. My friends were staying in a cabin and the door frame was all milduey as the gutters were over flowing due to not being cleaned. There was a sign at the campsite to indicate drinking water but the water was yellow/brown. They had just opened a new amenities block  and in the men's the urinal flushed constantly and each time it flushed the showers turned off. My brother informed management just so they were aware as it was a waste of water. His response was "well I can't do anything about it now" a more polite response would be "thanks for letting us know" this is a typical example of our experience with the staff.

Despite all this it was a great location, with lots of wildlife. And on the plus side staff don't care so you really have the campsite to your self to do as you wish, which worked as all the campers were respectful of each other.

We did enjoy our Xmas here.

This place has a lot of potentional  but really needs management who care and know what they are doing.

The bathroom of our cottage was not great, extremely basic. We couldn't sleep the first two nights due to mice noises coming from the walls and ceiling, we finally did sleep our last night as we we're all exhausted. The mice were still there thought.

My family and I thoroughly enjoyed our 3 night stay. We were a bit concerned about the rainy weather but it didn't seem to be an issue. The camp grounds were nice and open with a lovely breeze. The Lots were very spacious and didn't not feel like you were intruding on your neighbours. Camp site and facilities were well maintained. Will definitely be back.

Room very average , no phone service or internet at all and not even advertised as such. Uncomfortable beds , doors don’t lock properly , lights broken , very messy gardens , pool view room is not what is advertised at all. Staff don’t really know anything about the retreat and some are quite rude. Food is overpriced compared to other places around , sporting facilities very very average
All in all stay away from this place I won’t be back.

The website looks amazing. Unfortunately, does not match the accommodation! Was very old, smelly and run down. Hole in the bathroom roof, very dusty. Screen doors had no locks & flyscreens were ripped. Stains on lounge & furniture all old & scuffed. Poor customer service by staff. Very disappointing.

Always our number 1 choice in holiday destination. The staff are always so welcoming and helpful. Meals at the restaurant are fresh and delicious. Rooms are clean and well maintained. We are a large family of 8 and there is something for everyone. Love Noosa North Shore.

Terribly disappointing experience. Stayed in a lake view apartment - bathroom was mouldy, skylight was leaking, no bedding provided for the cot, the fan was faulty and not going higher than speed 1, the fridge was glued to a high temperature so our groceries went off after a couple of days.
They also don't make it clear at all that the only way to access the property is via a cash only ferry at $10 each way.

The place itself seems really cool with all the activities and a great pool, but the amenities and their service is really lacking.

The brand new Glamping tents were just gorgeous - attention to every detail was evident.  Best location for ease of access to a glorious beach/ lake and river.  Spacious sites so we didn't feel like we were on top of others.  Great pub and mini shop.  Thanks so much for another great stay Aaron & NNSR team.  L

Great camp grounds. We stayed in an unpowered site, but the sites are large and spacious. Plenty of grass cover and tree shade. Pool is a great hit for an arvo cool off and the amenities are outstanding. Hot/cold showers, flushing toilets that are well maintained. Definitely be back

We stayed a month including Christmas. Great location Tewantin town is lovely and close to Noosa etc. Ferry was fine and we missed the long queues. Park itself is spacious good sites. Glamping accommodation is good. We caravaned.

Yeah great place guys
Will be recommending to all our camper friends, who aren't doing camping again

My favorite place in Noosa
If you like the nature and. Quieter place .this will be your place.

Nice place with lovely facilities. The lake view room we had did not have air conditioning, making it really difficult to manage during the middle of summer. Otherwise, it would have been a perfect 10.

Just another day in Paradise with excellent coffee - Mara makes a mean latte in super quick time.
Also got a sneak peak at the new glamping tents.

A really nice place to stay. There is a beautiful restaurant and bar. If you are staying nearby they have the basics in the shop at reasonable prices. There is a bottle shop as well which again is not designed to rip you off. Alysse makes a very nice cappuccino too. Plenty of things for the kids to do as well as you enjoy a meal and a beer!

Our week at Noosa North Shore Retreat was an absolute dream. The quiet, expansive beach, stand-up paddleboarding at sunset on the nearby lake, and daily visits from kangaroos made it an unforgettable experience. Privacy was a plus throughout.

Days were filled with beach rides, paddleboarding, and poolside chilling. Kangaroos added a wild charm to our cabin surroundings. The onsite shop covered all essentials, and the beautifully done pub sealed the deal. Already planning our speedy return for more.

What a beautiful experience. Lounging by the beach everyday, stunning sunset views over the lake (30 seconds from the cabin) and cooling off in any of the pools was just divine. Kangaroos visited us every evening, just to top it all off.

We rode bikes down onto the beach, played games of putt putt on site and did stand up paddle boarding against the picture perfect lake at sunset.

Can't wait to go back.
New owners have done allot of work to improve the facilities.

Good toilets and showers. Campsites didn't have much shade at all and no privacy, sites are pretty bare and surrounded by road track rather than being tucked around the edges of the area or behind bushes/trees. In addition there was a very strong street light at night time about 10m away from our campsite which lit up the tent like day at night. Not the nicest. Good location at North shore which was why we booked, unfortunately we had car troubles and had to leave one night early to take the car direct to a mechanic, requested a refund or partial refund but told by the manager this was not possible despite the circumstances. Overall an average camping experience
Campsite was great.  Loved the late Sunday checkout.  Nice and quite and up at the hotel there was plenty of activities for kids.

The staff especially indoor bouldering staff are friendly, the lakeview unit is quite and tidy.
Recommended place to stay, just be aware if you choose to leave on Sunday try to leave earlier in the morning or later afternoon to avoid the long que wait for ferry. We've been waiting for about 1 hour to get on the ferry.

What is there not to love about nature, the beach and off peak stays away.
I stayed in a Safari Tent and it's only a 4 star review as it seemed to be incomplete and still undergoing renovation/upgrade along with all the others onsite. They look like they'll be really nice.
But the safari tent was clean, bedding very comfortable, close amenities block was very clean and park super clean and tidy.
Staff were friendly and very helpful.
I'd love to stay again when upgrades/renovations are all complete.
Very comfortable rooms with aircon. Excellent food and service! Chef Julian was so accomodating and went above and beyond for all!

I chose this camp spot as I was camping alone and wanted the reassurance of facilities in case all went wrong. Well ALL WENT WILDLY WRONG with a tent that broke in a gust of wind BUT the helpful staff lent me tools and a fellow camper helped me out and together we mended my tent and I had an amazing holiday with my gorgeous dog. I recommend this place but would've liked to know that it was ferry access only beforehand.
Highly recommend staying withthese guys. They are  very easy to work with and have looked out for us all the time. Such great experience ! Thank you! We come again!

Lots of accommodation choices from camping or caravan at the tourist park to cabins and dorms at the retreat. Spacious grounds, everything is being renovated. Shop and bottleshop 7am-3pm and the bar & bistro open Friday afternoon to Sunday afternoon and holidays. School camps run weekdays so on weekends you can book in and also hire gear like SUP and mountain bikes, ride the pump track or walk in the reserve, play mini golf, go indoor bouldering and there arcade games too.
Stayed at the camp site, excellent

Our stay in Cottage #4 was perfect! It was in a quiet location within the grounds and the cottage was very tidy inside. Check in/out was an easy process and friendly staff too. Dinner at the restaurant was also great!

I recently stayed at Noosa North Shore Caravan Park and had a mixed experience. The wildlife in the area was truly amazing, providing a unique and immersive natural setting. However, I was disappointed by the lack of maintenance in the park. The facilities, including the water outlets for caravans and the campsite areas, were non-existent, which would have been nice to know before making the booking, we would have come with full tanks. The faulty laundry equipment added to the inconvenience.

The lack of maintenance extended to the trees, which needed attention.

To make matters worse, the quality of the drinking water left much to be desired, with a cloudy yellow colour, and the facilities were appalling, yellow streaks on all toilet bowls and the tiles in the showers, and not not clean. The only positive is that they are currently being renovated.

The worse was being given the only site with not even a blade of grass, just dirt and sharp rock, the park was a quarter full and all other sites around us had grass...lol

While the park's natural setting is undoubtedly a draw, there is significant room for improvement in terms of maintaining facilities and ensuring a more pleasant camping experience.

I stayed at non-powered site, in a tent. The spot was flat, however, next to the fence with a large hole to a well lit back patio of some property, so it was not dark at night. The spot was not marked and tucked away behind the other spot, took me a while to find it. There was a lot of construction going on, no camp kitchen, so  come well prepared with own kettle and stuff. Alternatively 700 m walk to the actual resort will get you cafe and pub facilities. Toilets were very basic and you need to pay extra for hot showers (which is not stated when I booked). So for $40 a night I felt like it was not a good value for money at all.
No powered camping. So good. Grounds were immaculate. Bathrooms were perfect. Pools were clean and well maintained.  Had dinner at restaurant. It was amazing! Loved everything about it.

This was my first time to the retreat and I absolutely loved it.  I'm just sorry I didn't know about the place sooner.  The grounds are beautiful with plenty of room for lots of campers.  There is so much for kids to do I don't believe they could ever get bored.  The staff are friendly and welcoming.  I did feel the food was a bit overpriced however it was very tasty and great portion sizes.  I will definitely be back.
Thanks for a good stay team!

Upon arrival at our unit we were greeted with the most blood thirsty mosquitoes, the unit was full of them, no fly spray supplied. The unit was in a state of disrepair. There was no shower door, so the bathroom floor flooded. There were no bedside tables in the bedroom, no power points, no bedside lights, the kitchen had very poor lighting couldn't see to do the dishes, the kitchen table was wobbley, the couch was most uncomfortable and ripped, and free WiFi was barley accessible, the curtains all had holes I'm them, the swimming pool had bird poo all the way around the edges, we were denied an early departure refund. I would not recommend staying here at $215 a night

Spacious pool side rooms, comfy beds, lots of space, quiet and peaceful location. Nice activities for kids, beach is not too far , 1km. Enjoyed our stay, tnx.

The walk ways need better lighting, gas bbq burners not maintained well.

A great mini escape with the family. We loved it soo much we extended for another night. Reception staff were super helpful and we even managed to pick up a  reasonably priced fishing rod at the store. Loved watching the kangaroos bounce though the campsites. Thank you, we will be back.
We had a fantastic time here on a powered site over the holidays.  So many amenities, the kids had a blast!!
Great site, we were camping so hard to rate the room, thit was super packed which wasn't great and we weren't warned about the toilets being portable. but we love this place and will be back..

We had no internet service the whole stay , tennis court is great - all it needs is a floor redo, meals are a bit expensive as well as the bike hire is expensive..a spa and sauna would be great!!
We had animals in the roof at night kept me awake...
Fantastic staff and great place to stay. Will see you again soon. Recommended to all.
Such a beautiful place! Everyone were very nice and welcoming, especially Glen. Glen was very helpful and kind.

Great place was a camp ground 20 years ago and shut down but now reopen still under construction but is good , grounds and amenities are keep clean and dogs are welcome with you I will be back for another vacation with my caravan or tent
had a great weekend close to the beach and shops if needed.  i would recommend to anyone looking for a camping get away with your pooch. Amenities will be great when they are finished
We really loved staying here

Rooms are extremely dated and really let the place down.  We had a one-bedroom hotel room overlooking the pool. Was a nice spot but the room looked like they date back to the 80s and smelt old and way overpriced. Definitely do not recommend.
The main foyer and bar area has undergone a great renovation so hope they put some effort in to transform the rest of this very tired rundown place.

Not alot of for the kids to do at all.
No water available.
No drinking water.........
We went last Sunday and there were very limited meals and what they did offer was ridiculously expensive, $34 for a parmi and $28 just for a schnitty. Staff were amazing and drinks were reasonable.
We absolutely Loved it! Brilliant check in and friendly. Thanks so much! Can’t wait to return!

You need to take a ferry to access this property.  Fare is $10 for an ordinary vehicle each way, so it becomes an expensive stay if you are wanting to sight-see around the Sunshine Coast.  Also, meals are not always available, so you have to rough it sometimes.  When the meals are available, they are excellent.
Fantastic
